records.notes: On 24 April 2025, in Ton Pay village (Mohnyin township, Mohnyin district, Kachin state), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs at a clinic. The military conducted airstrikes a total of three times on 22, 24 and 26 April 2025 (coded separately). There were no casualties.

On 22 April 2025, in Ton Pay village (Mohnyin township, Mohnyin district, Kachin state), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs at a clinic around 11 pm. The military conducted airstrikes a total of three times on 22, 24 and 26 April 2025 (coded separately). There were no casualties.

On 16 April 2025, in Thit Seint village (coded as Thit Seint Gyi) (Wetlet township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes by dropping a 500lb bomb near a village clinic using a fighter jet from Tada-U airbase at 1:40 pm without an armed clash occurring. The bomb landed on the houses located in the west of the clinic, killing 4 civilians, including a 12-year-old boy, and injuring 6 others. Two houses were also damaged.

On 10 April 2025, in Kyonpyaw township (Pathein district, Ayeyarwady region), an unknown armed group shot and killed the Nga Gyi Htu village tract administrator around 2 pm while he returned from the township level meeting with a motorcycle. He was shot two times and died with injuries while on his way to the Yangon General Hospital. No group has claimed responsibility for the attack.

On 21 March 2025, in Sittwe town (Sittwe township, Sittwe district, Rakhine state), a 20-year-old man from Mi Zan ward who was arrested by the Myanmar military on 19 March 2025 (coded separately) died due to the beating and torture during the interrogation. He died while being taken to Sittwe General Hospital.

On 10 March 2025, in Sa Ne town (Kyaukpyu township, Kyaukpyu district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military troops from the Taung Maw Gyi navy base conducted a drone strike on an empty hospital, which was closed, at around 3:00 am. There were no casualties.

On 8 March 2025, in Htantabin township (Taungoo district, Bago-East region), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes on an emergency clinic run by the Free Burma Rangers, a multi-ethnic humanitarian service movement. A female medical officer of the Free Burma Rangers was killed while two female medical officer, including a 26-year-old medical officer, got injured. One of the injured medical officers later died from injuries on 10 March. Total fatalities coded as 2.

On 23 February 2025, in Nat Chaung village (Kale township, Kale district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs at the hospital around 8 am. The bombs fell and exploded on the hospital building where monks were undergoing medical treatment. Many houses in the village were also damaged. There were no casualties.

On 19 February 2025, in Nawnghkio town (Nawnghkio township, Kyaukme district, Shan-North state), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes on a public hospital, residential areas and a police station (assumed to be empty) at around 8:15 pm. 3 civilians were killed and 4 others were injured.

On 17 February 2025, in Tu Ywin Boet village (Myingyan township, Myingyan district, Mandalay region), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes and dropped 3 bombs at around 10:00 pm. A civilian house was destroyed. 3 civilians were killed and another civilian died from the injuries on the way to the hospital. At least 6 more civilians were injured.

On 14 February 2025, in Nat Chaung village (Kale township, Kale district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military forces conducted airstrikes by dropping bombs on the village clinic at around 10:20 pm. Casualties unknown.
On 14 February 2025, in Kyaw village (Kale township, Kale district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on a village clinic at about 6:47 pm. Three family members were killed.

On 14 February 2025, in Kale township (Kale district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs at the Station Hospital (Southern Kalaymyo) in the southern part of the township at around 10 am. There were no casualties.

On 13 February 2025, near Daw Yauk Khu village (coded as Daw Yauk Khu (South)) (Demoso township, Loikaw district, Kayah state), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes and dropped bombs with a fighter jet around 2:30 pm in the western part of the township. At least 7 buildings including a school and a clinic were destroyed. There were no casualties.

On 13 February 2025, in Kale township (Kale district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on the Golden Valley Revolution Hospital, which was run by resistance forces, at around 10:25 am. 5 volunteer health workers sustained injuries, and the hospital was damaged.

On 12 February 2025, in Yangon - Dawbon (Yangon-East district, Yangon region), a local man set fire to three election census workers, including a male 100-household head, a female 10-household head, and another woman, by dousing them with gasoline around 12:30 pm in the Dhamma Yeik Thar street of Kyi Su ward. They were injured and sent to the Yangon General Hospital. The 100-household head died on 26 February 2025, and the female 10-household head died on 2 March 2025.
On 31 January 2025, in Tin Thar village (Kale township, Kale district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes over a village clinic at about 5 pm, injuring some civilians.

On 30 January 2025, in Shauk Taw village (Pwintbyu township, Minbu district, Magway region), the Myanmar military arrested seven health workers, including two medical doctors, for unknown reasons. Locals assumed that they may have been arrested on accusations of having ties with resistance forces. The detainees were then taken to Pwintbyu town.
On 30 January 2025, in Singu town (Singu township, Pyinoolwin district, Mandalay region), the Myanmar military forces conducted airstrikes by dropping bombs on the Hospital. Casualties unknown.

On 22 January 2025, in Hpa Lan Taung village (Hpa-An township, Hpa-An district, Kayin state), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on the village hospital at night. The hospital was damaged by the airstrikes. There were no casualties.

records.actor: SSPP/SSA-N: Shan State Progress Party/Shan State Army-North
records.notes: On 15 January 2025, near Nam Mo village (Namhsan township, Pa Laung Self-Administered Zone, Shan-North state), fighting broke out between the Shan State Army-North (SSPP/SSA-N) and Shan State Army-South (RCSS/SSA-S) near the village. Heavy artillery shells fired during the fighting landed on a village clinic building. There were no casualties.
records.actor2: RCSS/SSA-S: Restoration Council of Shan State/Shan State Army-South

records.notes: On 3 January 2025, in Kanhtauntkyi town (Myebon township, Mrauk-U district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes two times and dropped bombs with a fighter jet at a high school and a hospital around 12 pm. A 70-year-old woman was severly injured and died with injuries. A school and a hospital were also destroyed.

records.actor: ULA/AA: United League of Arakan/Arakan Army
records.notes: On 18 December 2024, in Ann town (Ann township, Kyaukpyu district, Rakhine state), the Arakan Army (ULA/AA) continued attacking the Western Regional Military Command Headquarters (NaPaKha). During the fight, the military launched airstrikes on the battlefield while the AA returned fire using tanks it had seized from the military. About 30 military doctors surrendered by raising white flags while other military soldiers continued to fight back. Casualties unknown.

On 16 December 2024, in Taunggyi town (Taunggyi township, Taunggyi district, Shan-South state), around 10 police and the People's Militia Force arrested a Kachin ethnic woman who was a patient attendant at the Pan Hlaing hospital in Thit Taw ward around 10 pm. Locals claimed that she was arrested with an accusation of being a member of the Kachin Independence Army (KIO/KIA) medical corp. She was taken to Taunggyi Myoma police station.

records.actor: ULA/AA: United League of Arakan/Arakan Army
records.notes: On 15 December 2024, in Ann town (Ann township, Kyaukpyu district, Rakhine state), heavy artillery shells fired by the Arakan Army (ULA/AA) during the battle killed a Myanmar military commander of the No.2 Military Hospital (300 beds) under the Western Regional Military Command Headquarters (NaPaKha). The shelling also killed some nurses working in the hospital.

On 4 December 2024, outside Ye-U town (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military troops stationed at the traditional medicine hospital fired heavy weapons around 2 pm which fell and exploded into farms outside of the town. Casualties unknown.

records.actor: Wolf Guerrilla Local People's Defense Force Ye-U
records.notes: On 3 December 2024, in Ye-U town (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Wolf Guerrilla Local People's Defense Force Ye-U and the People's Defense Force - Ye-U attacked the Myanmar military troops stationed at the Ye-U traditional medicine hospital with four 60-mm heavy weapons around 7:30 pm. The military troops stationed in the traditional hospital and Kyar Phoo checkpoint at the town entrance returned fire with heavy weapons and small arms. A military soldier was severely injured and a house was destroyed.

records.actor: ULA/AA: United League of Arakan/Arakan Army
records.notes: On 30 November 2024, in Ann town (Ann township, Kyaukpyu district, Rakhine state), the Arakan Army (ULA/AA) attacked and captured all Myanmar military guard posts of the Western Regional Military Command, including those in Myo Thit ward, airport (Lay Yin Kwin) ward, Auk Ywar ward, at No.2 high school, and near the military hospital. The military launched airstrikes during the clashes, causing some houses to catch fire. Casualties unknown.

On 26 November 2024, in Kywe Ta Lin village of Kyaukkyi township (Taungoo district, Bago-East region), the Myanmar military carried out a drone strike at 8 am, destroying a hospital and two other buildings. There were no casualties.

On 26 November 2024, in Lumte village (Falam township, Falam district, Chin state), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes and dropped two bombs on a village clinic at about 2 pm without an armed clash occurring. Khit Thit Media, Democratic Voice of Burma, VOA, and Khonumthung News reported that 7 civilians, including 2 health workers, were killed while Mizzima, Myanmar Now, Radio Free Asia and Shwe Phee Myay News Agency reported that 6 civilians, including a female nurse and 5 male patients, were killed, and another sustained injuries. A clinic, a church, and about six houses were also damaged. Fatalities coded as 7, using the latest reports.

On 16 November 2024, in Taw Kyaung Pauk (coded as Taw Kyaung Pauk (Middle)) village (Kyaukkyi township, Taungoo district, Bago-East region), the Myanmar military troops from LID-264 carried out drone strikes, dropping bombs on a school building. The attack injured four local civilians, including two 10-year-old students. The strikes also caused significant damage, destroying a clinic, a kindergarten building, a church, and four residential houses.

On 14 November 2024, in Nawnghkio town (Nawnghkio township, Kyaukme district, Shan-North state), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes and dropped two bombs on a clinic in Zay ward and near the CB Bank using a fighter jet at around 4 pm. A man was killed, and the clinic was damaged.

records.actor: Taung Pan Ni Ah Htoo Pyauk Kyar Tatphwe: Red Wing Special Guerrilla Force
records.notes: On 13 November 2024, in Yinmarbin town of Yinmarbin township (Yinmarbin district, Sagaing region), a combined force of the Red Wing Special Guerrilla Force (People's Defense Force - Yinmarbin District Battalion-35 Company-3), the 5/Fighter (Monywa) (People's Defense Force - Yinmarbin District Battalion-35 Company-3 Squad-1), the Chindwin Dynamite and local resistance groups fired rounds of 60mm, 80mm and 120mm artillery at Myanmar military soldiers stationed at a military outpost, national park, district office, water resource gate, and Yinmarbin hospital at around 3:30 pm. The military fired back with 60mm, machine guns, and small weapons. Casualties unknown.

On 5 November 2024, in Maw Lu town (Indaw township, Katha district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs at the hospital around 3:30 pm. There were no casualties.

On 1 November 2024, in Kutkai town (Kutkai township, Muse district, Shan-North state), the Myanmar military forces conducted airstrikes by dropping 10 bombs on the Internal Revenue Office, Firefighter station, hospital, NaTaLa School building, electric power corporation (EPC) office, and near the Asia World Tollgate in No.2 ward using two fighter jets at around 3:28 am, 3:33 am, and 3:38 am. The airstrikes killed a member of the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA) and a local. Several other people sustained injuries.
records.actor2: PSLF/TNLA: Palaung State Liberation Front/Ta'ang National Liberation Army

records.notes: On 28 October 2024, in Dee Doke Kwin village (Pauk township, Pakokku district, Magway region), about 100 Myanmar military and Pyu Saw Htee in the People's Defense Force uniforms opened fire and raided an IDP camp and detained 17 civilians and a monk. A man was shot and killed and another one was burned to death in his house. Four other civilians were injured during the raid. Seven IDP houses and a clinic were set on fire, and villagers' property was looted.

On 28 October 2024, in Falam town (Falam township, Falam district, Chin state), an artillery shell fired by the Myanmar military landed on a house in Tlanglo ward at around 9 pm. A 38-year-old school teacher was killed. Khonumthung News reported that she was shot dead by a military troop with a sniper and a sniper bullet was removed from her body in the hospital.

On 23 October 2024, in Mandalay - Pyigyitagon (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), the police arrested a 67-year-old military physician who was also the director of Mingladon military hospital. He was also a former lieutenant colonel and son-in-law of the former commander in chief of the Myanmar military. The State Administration Council announced that he was arrested and detained with accusations of inciting instability of the state and disruption of government machinery through social media. He was transferred to the Insein prison from Oe Bo prison on 30 April, claiming that the remaining cases will be processed there.

records.actor: ULA/AA: United League of Arakan/Arakan Army
records.notes: On 19 October 2024, in Ann town (Ann township, Kyaukpyu district, Rakhine state), the Arakan Army (ULA/AA) attacked the Myanmar military hospital which is located three miles away from the Western Regional Military Command headquarters around 10 pm. The clash continued to 2 am the next day. The military responded by conducting airstrikes. Casualties unknown.

On 16 October 2024, in Kha Paung Kyaing village (Taze township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military continued to conduct an airstrike and dropped bombs around 2 am for the second day without an armed clash occurring. Some locals were injured. A clinic and monastery were also destroyed. 2 locals were injured on 15 and 16 October.

On 16 October 2024, in Kone Yoe village (Taze township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs at a clinic without an armed clash occurring. A local was injured and the clinic was destroyed.

On 16 October 2024, in Toungup town (Toungup township, Thandwe district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military arrested a 45-year-old woman who was a senior nurse from the Toungup district people's hospital in her house at the Tar Wa Tain Tar road in Kaing Shay ward around 6 pm. Locals claimed that she was arrested and detained at the No.5 military operations command with an accusation of being associated with the Arakan Army.

On 15 October 2024, in Kha Paung Kyaing village (Taze township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs around 11 pm for the first day without an armed clash occurring. Some locals were injured. A clinic and monastery were also destroyed. 2 locals were injured on 15 and 16 October.

On 13 October 2024, in Waing village (Okpho township, Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), the Myanmar military forces arrested about 20 young men near the village clinic at around 2 pm after the mine attack (coded separately).

records.actor: Gyobingauk Hero Pyauk Kyar Tatphwe: Gyobingauk Hero Guerrilla Force
records.notes: On 13 October 2024, in Waing village (Okpho township, Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), the Gyobingauk Hero Guerrilla Force (the People's Defense Force - Thayarwady District Battalion - 1 Company - 5) attacked a column of around 50 Myanmar military soldiers who were patrolling with four mines near a village clinic at about 1:45 pm. Khit Thit Media reported that over 10 military troops were killed and others were injured.

On 11 October 2024, in Min Kone village (Kanbalu township, Kanbalu district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military dropped six bombs on the village using drones at around 4:30 am, damaging a monastery and a clinic. On the same day, the Myanmar military set fire to houses, burning down 48 out of the 167 houses. There were no casualties.

On 8 October 2024, in Pan Ni Lar village (Ponnagyun township, Sittwe district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs near a temporary hospital with a fighter jet around 1:30 am. Separately, the No.12 police battalion based in Sittwe and Par Da Leik based police battalion fired heavy weapons indiscriminately till 3 am. There were no casualties.

On 7 October 2024, in La Maing Kawt village (Tanintharyi township, Myeik district, Tanintharyi region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs near a clinic. There were no casualties.

On 6 October 2024, in Ann town (Ann township, Kyaukpyu district, Rakhine state), seven Myanmar military and police arrested a 43-year-man at his house and took him away by putting a hood on his head around 7 pm in the Myo Thit - 3 ward. Locals claimed that he was arrested with an accusation of supporting medical supplies to the Arakan Army (ULA/AA). The whereabouts of him were unknown.

On 3 October 2024, in Maung Htaung village (Budalin township, Monywa district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes, dropping three bombs on a school and a hospital in the village. Casualties unknown.

On 1 October 2024, in Hpa Lu Gyi village (Myawaddy township, Myawaddy district, Kayin state), the Myanmar military stationed on the Swei Taw Kone hill fired seven 120 mm artillery rounds at around 2 pm and two of the rounds landed on the village school, damaging a building and a clinic in the school. VOA and Khit Thit Media reported that there were no casualties in the school. Another source, Mizzima reported that the artillery rounds also landed in Hpa Yar Nogke To pagoda, hitting and injuring a man.

On 26 September 2024, in Mandalay - Mahaaungmyay (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), more than 20 Myanmar military and police raided the Hla Ya Min hostel in the Daing Taw ward. They arrested a nurse who joined the civil disobedience movement (CDM).

On 22 September 2024, in Ma Au Auk Seik village (Yesagyo Township, Pakokku district, Magway region), the Myanmar military's fighter jet dropped two bombs on the village, destroying a sub-township hospital building, a monastery, the Salin Kan Lel stupa, a monastic building, and several houses, at around 8:30 am. The airstrike killed two girls, aged 23 and 17, and injured four women, aged 70, 50, 46, and 35.

On 5 September 2024, in Ba Hin village (Myaing township, Pakokku district, Magway region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs around 5 pm near a school and a hospital. A house and a car were destroyed. There were no casualties.

Around 4 September 2024 (as reported), in Lel Taung Yar village (Tanintharyi township, Myeik district, Tanintharyi region), the Myanmar military from the Wut Sin Taung pagoda hill fired heavy weapons five times around 8:20 pm without an armed clash occurring. The heavy weapons fell and exploded near a hut on a farm. A 40-year-old woman was severely injured and died with injuries at the Myeik Hospital. Khit Thit Media reported that the event occurred on 3 September and DVB reported that it occurred on 4 September. Mizzima reported that a woman was killed and some injured.

On 25 August 2024, in Myo Haung village (Toungup township, Thandwe district, Rakhine state), Myanmar military troops from the Toungup-based LIB-544 arrested a man by accusing him of providing medical supplies to the Arakan Army (ULA/AA). He was also accused of providing medical treatment to the ULA/AA, although a local source claimed that he was treating local people.

On 16 August 2024, in Kyeintali town (Gwa township, Thandwe district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs at the Kyeintali hospital. The hospital was damaged. Casualties unknown.

records.actor: ULA/AA: United League of Arakan/Arakan Army
records.notes: On 11 August 2024, in Kyeintali town (Gwa township, Thandwe district, Rakhine state), the Arakan Army (AA) attacked the military soldiers at the Kyeintali police station and two military camps. Both sides exchanged fire at night. During the fight, the military conducted airstrikes by dropping bombs with two jet fighters. Some of the bombs landed in the hilly area near the police station, while others hit the old police station near a hospital. Casualties unknown.

On 6 August 2024, in Mrauk-U town (Mrauk-U township, Mrauk-U district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military forces launched airstrikes using bombs on the township hospital at around 2:30 pm. Casualties unknown.

On 5 August 2024, in Let Pan Hla village (Singu township, Pyinoolwin district, Mandalay region), the Myanmar military forces launched airstrikes on the village in the evening, assuming local PDF might be located there. A primary school, private hospitals and some shops in Pyi Soe Aung ward were destroyed. DVB reported that 3 locals were injured.

On 4 August 2024, in Mongyai town (Mongyai township, Lashio district, Shan-North state), about 7 Myanmar military troops positioned near the hospital exchanged fire with the military troops on three military vehicles parked near the hospital at around 11 am due to miscommunication. A local civilian and three military soldiers were injured in the fighting.

On 2 August 2024, in Laukkaing town (Laukkaing township, Kokang Self-Administered Zone, Shan-North state), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on Ton Chaing and Kyin Mauk areas at around 1 am. A hospital in Ton Chaing ward was damaged by the airstrikes. At least 10 individuals, including pregnant women, hospital security and children, were killed.

records.actor: MNTJP/MNDAA: Myanmar National Truth and Justice Party/Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army
records.notes: On 1 August 2024, in Lashio town (Lashio township, Lashio district, Shan-North state), a combined force of the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNTJP/MNDAA) and allied forces attacked the Northeastern Regional Military Command Headquarters and raided the Myanmar military's No.9 100-bed hospital located within the compound of the Northeastern Regional Military Command. The combined force carried out drone attacks and fired rockets during the attack. Eleven Media Group and pro-military propaganda media channels claimed that over 100 individuals from the hospital, including doctors, nurses and patients, were killed during the raid. According to Shan Herald Agency for News, local sources claimed that those receiving treatment at the hospital were mostly wounded soldiers, and that they were killed during the raid. Khit Thit Media reported that at least 40 military troops, including high-ranking officers, were killed. Fatalities coded as 40 using the lowest estimates.

On 31 July 2024, in Hpone Nyo village (Toungup township, Thandwe district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military arrested two female nurses, a female teacher, and her son at the Hpone Nyo checkpoint as they were returning home after withdrawing their salaries in Toungup town. The arrestees were then detained at the LIB-544 base.

records.actor: MNTJP/MNDAA: Myanmar National Truth and Justice Party/Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army
records.notes: On 30 July 2024, in Lashio town (Lashio township, Lashio district, Shan-North state), joint forces of the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA) and the Bamar People's Liberation Army (BPLA) attacked the Northeastern Regional Military Command Headquarters with over 10 rounds of rockets at around 10 am. Fighting also broke out between the Myanmar military and the MNDAA at the Lashio airport, near the Northeastern Regional Military Command in the No.9 ward, near the No.626 Logistics and Supply Battalion, near the No.5 Basic Education High School, near the Artillery Battalion and near the Lashio based military hospital. During the armed clash, the military fired heavy weapons which fell and exploded into the No.9 and No.10 wards and some houses were destroyed. The MNDAA captured the Lashio airport. Irrawaddy reported that an officer from the artillery battalion died and two other soldiers were injured in the fighting.

On 29 July 2024, in Tatkon township (Oke Ta Ra district, Nay Pyi Taw region), the People's Defense Force - Myingyan District Battalion - 3 attacked the Myanmar military vehicle near the milestone-322 on the Yangon-Mandalay highway road with a mine around 8 am. 2 military soldiers were injured and a captain died with injuries when he arrived to the No.2 Military Hospital. A military vehicle was also destroyed.

On 26 July 2024, in Lashio town (Lashio township, Lashio district, Shan-North state), fighting broke out between the Myanmar military and the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNTJP/MNDAA) troops in No.4, No.5, No.9, No.11, No.12 wards, and near the Man Su pagoda, Lashio town hall, Lashio hospital and Lashio motel. During the fight, the MNDAA conducted drone strikes on the Lashio town hall, hitting and injuring a brigadier general and a police officer, and the military conducted an airstrike and fired heavy weapons which exploded into the Ma Har Byu Har Street in No.8 ward, the No.4 A Way Yar Street, No.1 ward, No.12 ward and Parami ward. 5 locals at the No.4 A Way Yar Street near the Man Su monastery were killed. Khit Thit Media reported that 2 young ladies in the Parami ward were also killed and 2 other locals were severely injured. Khit Thit Media reported that the commander of the No.10 Field Medical Battalion who fled the fighting was shot and killed during the escape, and a militia member was also killed in the crossfire at a location between the Northeastern Regional Military Command Headquarters and the AD traffic light intersection. Total fatalities coded as 9 (including 2 military soldiers and 7 civilians).
records.actor2: MNTJP/MNDAA: Myanmar National Truth and Justice Party/Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army

records.notes: On 16 July 2024, in Singu town (Singu township, Pyinoolwin district, Mandalay region), the People's Defense Force - Mandalay attacked the Myanmar military in the town. During the armed clash, the military conducted airstrikes more than 20 times and dropped bombs with a fighter jet from 12 pm to 9 pm and fired heavy weapons. Around 600 houses in the No.1 ward, No.2 ward and some buildings in the Singu hospital were destroyed and some locals were killed. DVB reported that some locals were killed and injured, while RFA reported that more than 20 locals were injured. Myanmar Now reported that two men were killed within two days due to the heavy weapons in the Shan Taw Waing area of the No.1 ward. 2 fatalities split across two days (15 - 16 July), and coded as 1 for this event.

records.actor: ULA/AA: United League of Arakan/Arakan Army
records.notes: On 15 July 2024, in Thandwe town (Thandwe township, Thandwe district, Rakhine state), the Arakan Army (ULA/AA) attacked the Thandwe prison where the Myanmar military and police troops were stationed. Around 50 military and police troops were captured as prisoners of war and AA troops released more than 100 prisoners and political prisoners who were detained at the prison. During the armed clash, the military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs which fell and exploded near a car workshop near the prison. Some locals were killed. The Thandwe district hospital, No.2 Basic Education High School, some pagodas and houses were destroyed. There were an unspecified number of fatalities. Unknown fatalities coded as 3.

On 14 July 2024, in Ho Peik village (Lashio township, Lashio district, Shan-North state), the Myanmar military fired rockets which hit and exploded a clinic and a house. Some locals were killed. There were an unspecified number of fatalities. Unknown fatalities coded as 3.

On 10 July 2024, in Hta Naung Kone village (Budalin township, Monywa district, Sagaing region), the military soldiers conducted a drone strike by dropping 6 bombs on the checkpoint of a local people's defense force on the Monywa - Ye-U road at around 10 am. They carried out another drone strike by dropping 6 more bombs on a medical clinic where the injured people's defense force members were being treated at around 11 am. Myanmar Now reported that one CDM health worker and 5 members of the local people's defense force were killed, and 15 others were severely injured. RFA reported that one female health worker and seven members of the local people's defense force were killed, and at least ten others were injured. DVB reported that one female health worker and five members of the local people's defense force were killed, and eight others were injured. Fatalities coded as 6, using the lowest estimate.

On 4 July 2024, in Lay village (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military stationed at the Ye-U traditional medicine hospital fired artillery rounds into the village at about 7:45 pm. A 40-year-old woman was killed by an artillery shell.

On 2 July 2024, in Kyu village (coded as Kyu-Ywar Ma) (Yesagyo township, Pakokku district, Magway region), about 50 Myanmar military soldiers coming from Yesagyo town raided a village clinic stationed by members of the Dragon Brothers Guerrilla Force at around 6 am. The two sides then exchanged fire. A member of PDF was killed during the armed clash. 3 members of PDF were captured, taken to the monastery, tortured and killed by the military. 2 members of PDF were also injured. The military troops seized 7 weapons, ammunition and communication devices.
records.actor2: Na Gar Nyi Naung Pyauk Kyar Tatphwe: Dragon Brothers Guerrilla Force

records.notes: On 29 June 2024, in War Bo village (Sittwe township, Sittwe district, Rakhine state), the military from the IB-232 base and the No.373 artillery base fired some artillery rounds at noon, suspecting that AA had entered the villages in the township. A village school, a hospital and some houses were destroyed. Casualties unknown.

records.actor: PSLF/TNLA: Palaung State Liberation Front/Ta'ang National Liberation Army
records.notes: On 29 June 2024, in Nawnghkio township (Kyaukme district, Shan-North state), joint forces of the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA), the People's Defense Force - Mandalay, and the Danu People's Liberation Front (DPLF) attacked the Myanmar military No.606 artillery battalion at around 4:30 am and IB-146 stationed at Shu Khin Thar at around 7 am. Following the clash, the military conducted airstrikes by dropping 500lb bombs with Mi-35 aircraft near the fire station at around 10:30 pm, hitting and destroying a clinic beside a fire station, stores and over 10 houses. Consecutively, the military also conducted drone strikes by dropping six bombs, hitting and destroying the Gant Gaw monastery. Ten military soldiers died in the fighting. The joint forces seized eight pistols, eight MA-1 rifles, three MA-3 rifles, two G-3 rifles, fifteen other rifles, ten carbines, three MA-15 machine guns, one M79 rifle, one RPG, four commando mortars, a 12mm gun, two 81 mm guns, two 60 mm heavy weapons, two jammers, and other military equipment. The TNLA claimed that the attack was carried out as Operation 1027 Part 2.

records.actor: KIO/KIA: Kachin Independence Organization/Kachin Independence Army
records.notes: On 26 June 2024, in Seik Mu (Hseng Taung) village (Hpakant township, Mohnyin district, Kachin state), a combined force of the KIA and the Kachin Region People's Defense Force attacked the Seik Mu (Hseng Taung) police station with bombs attached to drones in the morning while Myanmar military troops and police were having a meeting. 10 military troops and police, including a police battalion commander, were killed, and six others sustained injuries. According to Kachin News Group, two of the injured soldiers later died at a hospital. The Myanmar military troops fired back heavy artillery shells, killing a local civilian and injuring eight others based on the RFA. DVB, Kachin News Group, Khit Thit Media, Mizzima and Shwe Phee Myay News Agency reported that artillery shells fired by the military landed on a house and a football field at around 10 am, killing a 20-year-old man and injuring seven others. Total fatalities coded as 13 (12 police and 1 civilian).

records.actor: PSLF/TNLA: Palaung State Liberation Front/Ta'ang National Liberation Army
records.notes: On 25 June 2024, in Nawnghkio town (Nawnghkio township, Kyaukme district, Shan-North state), a combined force of the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA) and the PDF (under the NUG) attacked the Myanmar military, police and People's Militia Forces stationed at LIB-115, LIB-114, a tollgate at the entrance of the town, the office of the Department of Meteorology and Hydrology, a hospital, the fire service department, a veteran office and a police station. The TNLA and allies captured the Department of Meteorology and Hydrology office, the hospital and the fire service department. The TNLA claimed that the attack was carried out as Operation 1027 Part 2. 7 military soldiers were killed and many were injured in the LIB-115 as the TNLA and local PDF troops attacked with rockets.

records.actor: PSLF/TNLA: Palaung State Liberation Front/Ta'ang National Liberation Army
records.notes: On 25 June 2024, in Kyaukme town (Kyaukme township, Kyaukme district, Shan-North state), a combined force of the TNLA and allied forces attacked the Myanmar military LIB-501, LIB-502, the artillery unit No.352, the Kyaukme Myoma police station and Myanmar military troops based in government offices and a public hospital around 10 am. The two sides exchanged fire with small weapons and artillery rounds during the clash. Some artillery shells landed in Zaw Ti Kone ward, Nyein Chan Yae Kone ward, Pang Pawng village and wards 1, 7, 8, and 9, killing two people and injuring four others. The military also carried out airstrikes, damaging a Buddhist monastery in Nyein Chan Ye Kone ward. The TNLA claimed that the attack was carried out as Operation 1027 Part 2. VOA and Shan Herald Agency for News reported that at least 21 people died during four days of fighting (25-28 June). Fatalities coded as 2 for this event, using the exact number of people who died on 25 June.

On 22 June 2024, between Ma Gyi Tha Hmyar village and Myingyan town (coded as Ma Gyi Tha Hmyar) (Myingyan township, Myingyan district, Mandalay region), the Myanmar military troops lying in wait near the No.18 ward shot and killed a monk who was driving a charity car and transporting a patient to the Myingyan hospital around 9:30 pm. A local who was in the car was also injured. DVB and RFA reported that the event occurred on the night of 22 June 2024 and VOA reported it occurred on 23 June 2024. Aid Worker Security Database reported that it was a patient who was shot and killed. Event date is coded as 22 June according to many sources. TP coded as 1.

On 14 June 2024, in Tanintharyi township (Myeik district, Tanintharyi region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs at a hospital of the Karen National Defence Organization Battalion - 7. A nurse was killed. VOA reported that a nurse was killed and KIC reported that a nurse and a woman were killed. Fatalities coded 1 as the lowest estimate.
On 7 June 2024, in Matupi town (Matupi township, Matupi district, Chin state), the Myanmar military arrested some locals and detained them at a hospital for interrogation.

On 4 June 2024, in Khin Shey (Taung Su) village (Pauk township, Pakokku district, Magway region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped 500-lb bombs around 10 am without an armed clash occurring which fell and exploded on a clinic. A clinic and some houses nearby were destroyed. Separately, the military troops stationed in the No.24 military equipment factory fired heavy weapons. 2 locals were injured and some cows were killed. Locals were forced to flee as the military conducted airstrikes and fired heavy weapons.

On 2 June 2024, in Myo Kone village (Shwegu township, Bhamo district, Kachin state), the military conducted airstrikes on the village hospital at around 1 am, hitting and destroying the hospital. Myanmar Now reported that an 8-year-old child was killed and at least 6 other locals, including the child's father, were injured. Khit Thit Media reported that a 9-year-old child was killed and more than 17 locals, including the hospital staff and patient attendants, were injured.

records.actor: KIO/KIA: Kachin Independence Organization/Kachin Independence Army
records.notes: On 2 June 2024, near Maw Hpawng village (Myitkyina township, Myitkyina district, Kachin state), a combined force of the KIA and their allied resistance forces attacked the Nam Paung military air base with two rockets at around 2:30 pm, hitting and destroying a clinic in the air base. Casualties unknown.
On 15 May 2024, in Maungdaw town (Maungdaw township, Maungdaw district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military arrested two men who were staff from the Maungdaw hospital near the downtown clocktower.

On 13 May 2024, in Myo Thit village (Momauk township, Bhamo district, Kachin state), the military conducted airstrikes on the village, hitting and destroying a juice shop near the village hospital at around 8:00 pm. A 40-year-old owner was killed, and the owner's wife and his mother in law were injured.

On 8 May 2024, in Yae Nge (Ya Nge) village (Thayetchaung township, Dawei district, Tanintharyi region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs in the evening. An elderly woman was killed and a hospital was destroyed.

On 7 May 2024, in Myo Thit village (Momauk township, Bhamo district, Kachin state), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs at the Myo Thit village hospital around 7 am. The hospital meeting hall was destroyed and some houses nearby the hospital were damaged. There were no casualites.

On 28 April 2024, in Vawmm'tu village (Mindat township, Mindat district, Chin state), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike with a fighter jet and dropped bombs at the Vawmm'tu hospital around 1:30 pm for the second time. The hospital and eight houses in the village were destroyed. There were no casualties as locals were able to flee.

On 28 April 2024, in Lun Don village (Kanpetlet township, Mindat district, Chin state), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes two times by dropping tree bombs at around 1:30 pm. A 45-year-old woman and an 18-year-old woman were injured and a 68-year-old man was killed. A village clinic and three houses were also destroyed.

On 28 April 2024, in Mansi town (Mansi township, Bhamo district, Kachin state), an unidentified armed group opened fire at a 40-year-old woman in front of a hospital at around 9 pm. The woman sustained injuries and was taken to the Bhamo general hospital for medical treatment.

On 25 April 2024, in Vawmm'tu village (Mindat township, Mindat district, Chin state), the military conducted airstrikes on two buildings of the Vawmm'tu hospital by dropping eight 500lb bombs with Jet Fighters two times starting from around 8 pm, hitting and destroying both hospitals. Khit Thit Media and Irrawaddy reported 4 locals were killed and at least other locals were injured. VOA reported that 4 locals were killed and 15 other locals were injured. Myanmar Pressphoto Agency reported that 4 locals were killed and 30 other locals were injured. DVB and Radio Free Asia updated on 27 April that one more injured local died. A total of fatalities coded as 5 for the most up-to-date estimates.

On 23 April 2024, in Hnaw Kone village (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military stationed at the Hnaw Kone hospital fired 81 mm artillery rounds on Hnaw Kone village while villagers were celebrating an almsgiving ceremony. Some houses were destroyed. There were no casualties.

On 19 April 2024, in War Dat Kwin village (Kyaikto township, Thaton district, Mon state), two unknown armed men shot and killed a 50-year-old man who was a leader of the local People's Militia Force who was extorting money from locals around 9 am. He was injured and died at a hospital. No group claimed responsibility for the attack.
records.actor2: Military Forces of Myanmar (2021-) People's Militia Force

records.notes: On 14 April 2024, in Shar Hla village of Seikphyu township (Pakokku district, Magway region), the Myanmar military forces conducted airstrikes on the village by dropping a bomb using a helicopter gunship while villagers were celebrating the Thingyan festival at around 5 pm and killed a 53-year-old woman. The airstrikes also damaged a school building, a clinic, and seven houses in the village.

On 13 April 2024, in Ma Le Thar village (Ayadaw township, Monywa district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on a hospital, a school, and a monastery using four helicopter gunships at around 4 pm, assuming that there were injured PDF members at the hospital. A medical doctor was killed.

On 28 March 2024, in Myaing town (Myaing township, Pakokku district, Magway region), the combined troops of the People's Defense Force - Myaing (PaKaPha), Ko Bar Kya Aphwe (Myaing), Myaing Villages People's Revolution Force and allies attacked the Myanmar military troops stationed at the Bus terminal and the town hospital around 2 pm. The armed clash took around an hour and the combined troops set fire and destroyed a building in the hospital and an immigration office where the military troops were stationed. Following the clash, the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike with a fighter jet coming from the Northwest Military Command. 2 members of the combined troops were injured and some military soldiers were killed. There were an unspecified number of fatalities. Unknown fatalities coded as 3.

Around 25 March 2024 (as reported), in Maungdaw township (Maungdaw district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military arrested and killed the Maungdaw district hospital superintendent and the physician with an accusation of maintaining links with the Arakan Army.

On 24 March 2024, in Kanhtauntkyi town (Myebon township, Mrauk-U district, Rakhine state), the military conducted airstrikes and some of the bombs landed on a school and the Myauk Kyaung monastery and Kan Htaunt Gyi Hospital at around 4 am, hitting and injuring a 40-year-old female health worker.

On 23 March 2024, in Nan Toke village (Pinlaung township, Pa-O Self-Administered Zone, Shan-South state), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on the village twice using a fighter jet and then fired five artillery rounds into the village. A clinic, a shop, and six houses were damaged. Casualties unknown.

On 23 March 2024, in Yesagyo town (Yesagyo township, Pakokku district, Magway region), the combined troops of the Myingyan Black Tiger, Popa Production & Guerrilla Force, People's Defense Force - Nyaung-U District Battalion - 1 Company - 1, Ah Nyar Myay Local Defense Force, People's Defense Force - Nyaung-U District Battalion - 1 Drone Force, AMA People's Defense Force, Peacock Warrior Myaung Guerrilla Force, YSO TPG Urban Guerrilla Force, Myingyan District Drone Strike No.1848, Shar Htoo Waw Drone Technical Team and Thway Thauk Revolution Force attacked the Myanmar military stationed at the Kat Kyaw monastery, General Administration Department office, Nyein Zar Ni veteran office and a hospital. During the armed clash, the military fired heavy weapons which fell and exploded into the No.8 ward around 6 am. A 30-year-old man was killed on the spot and two women, a 37-year-old and a 54-year-old died at a hospital with injuries. A monk and a woman were also injured. 7 military soldiers were killed and 8 were severely injured.

On 22 March 2024, in La Ei village of Pekon township (Taunggyi district, Shan-South state), the military conducted airstrikes by dropping 120 lb bombs four times on the 16-Bed General Hospital at around 10:10 am. The hospital and an ambulance were destroyed. Khit Thit Media reported 2 locals were injured. Another source, Radio Free Asia reported a 16-year-old and a 40-year-old were killed and 3 other locals were injured. Another source, Shan Herald Agency for News reported a 50-year-old IDP man and a 20-year-old man were killed and 3 other locals were injured. Fatalities coded as 2 for the most up-to-date estimates.

On 22 March 2024, in Nan Toke village (Pinlaung township, Pa-O Self-Administered Zone, Shan-South state), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped two 500 lb bombs without an armed clash occurring around 11:52 pm and 11:58 pm. Some houses and a clinic in the village were destroyed. There were no casualties.

On 20 March 2024, in Kaing Taw Ma village of Myaing township (Pakokku district, Magway region), joint forces of the People's Defense Force - Myaing (PaKaPha) (B-1 and IO Group), Wolf Guerrilla Force - Myaing, Myaing Underground Defense Force (MUGDF), James Bond Guerrilla Force, Zarmani Guerrilla Force - Myaing, Kin Do People's Defense Force, People's Defense Force - Pakokku District Battalion (2, 6, 7), People's Defense Force - Pakokku, Young Force-UG, and Earthquake People's Defense Force carried out an attack against about 12 Myanmar military troops in front of Kaing Taw Ma village clinic at around 11:30 am. Both sides exchanged fire for 15 minutes. The joint forces seized an MA-1 rifle, an MA-3 rifle, six boxes of magazines, 120 rounds of bullets, and two mobile phones. There were 3 fatalities and 2 injuries on the military side.

On 17 March 2024, near Haing Yu village (Minhla township, Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes with bombs attached to drones. A school, a clinic, two churches, a monastery and 110 houses were destroyed during five days of airstrikes (13 - 17 March). Casualties unknown.

On 16 March 2024, in Haing Yu village (Minhla township, Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes with bombs attached to four drones and fired heavy weapons. A school, a clinic, two churches, a monastery and 110 houses were destroyed during five days of airstrikes (13 - 17 March). Casualties unknown.

On 15 March 2024, near Haing Yu village of Minhla township (Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), fighting broke out between military forces and the People's Defense Force - Thayarwady District Battalion - 3802 Company - 5. Three members of the PDF Thayarwady District Battalion-3082 Company-5 were injured. On the same day, the Myanmar military dropped bombs the village using four drones. A school, a clinic, two churches, a monastery and 110 houses were destroyed during five days of airstrikes (13 - 17 March).

On 14 March 2024, in Haing Yu village (Minhla township, Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes with bombs attached to four drones and fired heavy weapons. A school, a clinic, two churches, a monastery and 110 houses were destroyed during five days of airstrikes (13 - 17 March). Casualties unknown.

records.actor: Ye Bi Lu Aphwe: Ye Ogre Group
records.notes: On 14 March 2024, near Chaung Taung village (Ye township, Mawlamyine district, Mon state), the Ye Ogre Group attacked the Myanmar military checkpoint where around 15 military soldiers were stationed at the Chaung Taung bridge around 8:30 am. 2 military soldiers were killed and 4 were severely injured. A member of the Ye Ogre Group and a woman who was passing nearby was also injured in the crossfire and sent to the hospital. The Ye Ogre Group claimed that this attack was part of Operation Ogre's Road.

On 13 March 2024, in Haing Yu village (Minhla township, Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), the Myanmar military conducted airstrikes with bombs attached to four drones and fired heavy weapons. A school, a clinic, two churches, a monastery and 110 houses were destroyed during five days of airstrikes (13 - 17 March). Casualties unknown.

On 9 March 2024, in Myitkyina town (Myitkyina township, Myitkyina district, Kachin state), an unidentified armed group fired artillery rounds and some of the rounds landed between the Myitkyina airport and the Myitkyina hospital in the morning, hitting and destroying a Jet and an airplane. Casualties unknown.

On 8 March 2024, in Ywar Thit village of Myawaddy township (Myawaddy district, Kayin state), the Myanmar military forces launched airstrikes on the village at around 11 am. The airstrikes landed on an aged-care clinic, killing two 50-year-old male civilians and injuring eight other elderly people. DVB reported that 3 elderly people were killed and eight other locals were injured. Fatalities coded as 2 for the lowest estimates.

records.actor: Police Forces of Myanmar (2021-) Prison Guards
records.notes: On 6 March 2024, in Kyaik Sa Kaw village of Daik-U township (coded as Kyaik Sa Kaw (East)) (Bago district, Bago-East region), a 66-year-old prisoner from Kyaik Sa Kaw Prison, who was arrested at his house on 3 October 2023 (coded separately), died from constipation after the prison guard refused to approve medical treatment at an outside hospital in time.

Property destruction: On 1 March 2024, in Khoe Than village (Monywa township, Monywa district, Sagaing region), the military raided the village in the morning and set fire to about 95 houses, a clinic, and a car.

On 27 February 2024, in Ka Nyin Kyoe village (Kyauktaga township, Bago district, Bago-East region), the military from the (IB-264) base fired three 120 mm artillery rounds on the village, hitting and destroying a clinic and two residential houses. Casualties unknown.

On 27 February 2024, in Min Hpu village of Minbya township (Mrauk-U district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military forces launched airstrikes using fighter jets on the Min Hpu village hospital at around 1:45 am. The airstrikes seriously injured over twenty patients and health workers inside the hospital.

On 12 February 2024, between Wet Tae Kwe and Pyawbwe villages (coded as Pyawbwe) (Kawa township, Bago district, Bago-East region), the People's Defense Force - Kawa shot and killed a Pyu Saw Htee who was riding a tractor around 9:30 am. A man who was riding on the same tractor with a member of Pyu Saw Htee was injured during the crossfire and died on his way to hospital.

Around 12 February 2024 (as reported), in Kale town (Kale township, Kale district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military killed a 25-year-old man who was a student from the Christian Theological Bible School. He was arrested by the military on 10 February and killed during the interrogation. The military cremated his body at the Kale hospital on 12 February.

On 9 February 2024, in Koke Ko Kone village of Kawlin township (Kawlin district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military forces launched four airstrikes on the village hospital located on Shwebo-Myitkyina road at around 3 pm. The airstrikes damaged the hospital building. Myaelatt Athan reported that 2 women were injured.

On 8 February 2024, in Za Yat Gyi town (Htantabin township, Yangon-North district, Yangon region), fighting continued between the Myanmar military troops and joint forces of the Karen National Liberation Army (KNLA), Bamar People's Liberation Army (BPLA), and the People's Defense Force (PDF under NUG). The Myanmar military launched airstrikes on the town. Some pagodas, a monastery and houses caught fire. During the airstrikes, about 20 civilians were stuck in the hospital. Three bodies of the women were found in Kyon Par ward.
records.actor2: KNU/KNLA: Karen National Union/Karen National Liberation Army

records.notes: Property destruction: On 5 February 2024, in Thea Kone Gyi village (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military Bulldog column of around 100 soldiers advanced from a traditional medicine hospital in Ye-U township and set fire to houses in the village.

On 28 January 2024, in Kyauk Hlay Khar village (Yesagyo township, Pakokku district, Magway region), an unidentified armed group opened fire at an ambulance belonging to the Phyu Sin charity organization on the Yesagyo-Chaung-U road at around 3 pm. The driver and his assistant managed to escape. The group then set fire to the ambulance. According to locals, the organization was once criticized by locals for allegedly supporting the military.

On 27 January 2024, in Son Kone village (Shwegyin township, Bago district, Bago-East region), three 81mm artillery shells fired by the Myanmar military struck a clinic in the evening. A child of a health worker and a 14-year-old girl from a nearby house sustained injuries.

On 19 January 2024, in Khin-U town (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), a combined force of local defense forces attacked Myanmar military troops stationed at a hospital with bombs attached to drones at around 12 pm. On the same day, the combined force attacked military troops with two bombs attached to drones while they were at the Min Theik tea shop. Three military troops were killed and four injured. The combined force includes the Eagle Guerrilla Force - Shwebo, the Kyaukse Red Dragon Guerrilla Force, the Lightning Drone Force (We Are Everywhere), the Future Hero, the Khin-U Area 3 Group, the People's Defense Force - Tabayin (PaKaPha) and the Flying Drone Force.

On 4 January 2024, in Moke Pa Lin village (Kyaikto township, Thaton district, Mon state), two members of an unidentified armed group on a motorcycle opened fire on a man at a betel nut shop at noon. The gunshots hit and injured the man, who later died from his injuries on the way to the hospital. A woman who was having lunch in front of a nearby house was also hit by gunfire and died on the spot. Fatalities coded as 2.

On 27 December 2023, in Samee town (Paletwa township, Matupi district, Chin state), a combined force of the Chinland Defense Force - Paletwa (Battalion 1) and the Eagle Force attacked a Myanmar military base located near the Samee General Hospital at about 4 pm. The two sides then exchanged fire for about an hour. The military troops were forced to retreat to Samee strategy hilltop base. Casualties unknown.

On 18 December 2023, in Nyaungshwe town (Nyaungshwe township, Taunggyi district, Shan-South region), a 25-year-old man from Nyaungshwe town, who was arrested on 9 December with the accusation of belonging to an unidentified armed group, died at Khine Metta Hospital hospital after sustaining severe injuries from torture during interrogation.

On 18 December 2023, in Bago town (Bago township, Bago district, Bago-East region), a 50-year-old man from Mu Du village of Kawa township, who was arrested by the Myanmar military along with other villagers on 27 April 2023, died from torture during interrogation. The man was arrested with the accusation of having ties with the PDF. He died at a hospital on 18 December 2023, and there were signs of torture and bruises on his body.

On 18 December 2023, in Namhsan town (Namhsan township, Pa Laung Self-Administered Zone, Shan-North state), the military conducted airstrikes two times in front of the general hospital at around 3 pm, hitting and destroying some houses. Casualties unknown.

On 16 December 2023, in Namhsan town (Namhsan township, Pa Laung Self-Administered Zone, Shan-North state), the military conducted airstrikes by dropping 500 lb bombs with a jet fighter at around 4:30 pm and some of the bombs landed on the main road in front of the general hospital, hitting and killing a Man Lone villager, an 18-year-old, a 22-year-old and a 33-year-old Zei Ban Kauk villager.

On 14 December 2023, in Pa Lu Za Wa village (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on the village using two Mi-35 helicopter gunships at 10:45 am. A monastery caught fire, and a hospital, a clinic, a school, and some houses were damaged. A member of an unidentified local defense force sustained critical injuries.

Around 12 December 2023 (as reported), in Htee Saung village (Myinmu township, Sagaing district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military stationed at the Eye Hospital on the Monywa-Mandalay road fired heavy weapons toward the village. Casualties unknown.

On 8 December 2023, in Paletwa town (Paletwa township, Matupi district, Chin state), the military (LIB-289) fired about 15 artillery rounds at around 10:48 am and some of the rounds landed on the hospital staff housing, hitting and destroying the roofs and injuring a 6-year-old girl.

On 7 December 2023, in Sittwe town (Sittwe township, Sittwe district, Rakhine state), the military arrested two male health workers from a hospital, who came out to buy betel nuts in Kyay Pin Gyi ward at around 10 pm. The two health workers were being detained at the No.1 police station.
On 4 December 2023, in Nyaung Pin Wun village (Sagaing township, Sagaing district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military arrested a doctor and took him to LID-33 based in Sagaing town.

On 3 December 2023, in Kha Ywea village of Waw township (Bago district, Bago-East region), the Myanmar military based in Ah Htet Kwayt Gyi and Let Pan villages fired 120mm and 80mm artillery rounds into the village at around 12 pm. Artillery shells landed in Htin Te ward, damaging a clinic, three monasteries, and two houses. Casualties unknown.

On 30 November 2023, in Ywar Shey village (Taze township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military conducted airstrikes by dropping two 500lb bombs on the village at around 2 am. One of the bombs landed in a Mango farm and another landed on a school, destroying a school building and some roofs of a hospital nearby. There were no casualties.

On 28 November 2023, in Wein Mai village of Kutkai township (Muse district, Shan-North state), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on the village, damaging a school, a hospital, an EPC office and about 20 houses. Casualties unknown.

On 26 November 2023, in Man Awng village (Namhkan township, Muse district, Shan-North state), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on a hospital with a fighter jet at around 2 am. According to the DVB, a 30-year-old woman and her four-year-old child were killed, while the RFA, the Shwe Phee Myay News Agency and Myanmar Pressphoto Agency reported that they sustained injuries. Fatalities coded as 0 using the lowest estimates.

On 23 November 2023, in Ponnagyun town (Ponnagyun township, Sittwe district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military fired artillery rounds into the town at night. An artillery shell landed in the compound of the Shwe Oo Yit Mogoke monastery, while another shell landed on a house near the Shwe Taung Tan monastery and the third shell landed on a house near a hospital. Some houses were damaged, and locals were forced to flee. A woman sustained injuries.

On 10 November 2023, in Khin-U town (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military stationed in Myo Ma police station, the Min Thaik building, and a hospital arrested a local in Mya Kan Thar ward in the evening for unknown reasons.

On 10 November 2023, in Ngar Yant Oh village (Myaung township, Sagaing district, Sagaing region), the military conducted airstrikes by dropping two bombs and firing machine guns from the aircraft in the evening. One of the air bombs landed on a clinic, destroying a clinic and a house. Casualties unknown.

On 9 November 2023, in Thone Se village (coded as Thone Se Zay Kone) (Nawnghkio township, Kyaukme district, Shan-North state), the military fired artillery rounds at around 3 pm and three of the rounds landed in the village, hitting and destroying a clinic and houses and injuring a woman.

On 4 November 2023, in Mawlamyine town (Mawlamyine township, Mawlamyine district, Mon state), the Myanmar military arrested a 29-year-old man for taking videos of barricades around the ministerial housing complex and allegedly sharing fake news and posts related to politics on TikTok. The man was arrested while he was at a teashop near a traditional hospital. He was charged under Section 505(a) of the Penal Code.

On 1 November 2023, in Let Yet Kone village (Tabayin township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military based in Ye-U traditional hospital fired artillery rounds at around 5 pm and some of the rounds landed on a house in the village, killing one and injuring 6 locals. Khit Thit Media reported another injured local also passed away during the treatment. Fatalities coded as 2.

On 27 October 2023, in Nyaung Pin Thar village (Tigyaing township, Katha district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on a hospital using two fighter jets from Myitkyina at around 3 pm. Casualties unknown.
Property destruction: On 25 October 2023, in Thea Inn village (Singu township, Pyinoolwin district, Mandalay region), the Myanmar military set fire to a village clinic and five houses.

On 21 October 2023, in Nyaung Kone (Upper) village of Mogoke township (Pyinoolwin district, Mandalay region), fighting broke out between about 200 Myanmar military LIB - 88, 104, 102 and LID-44 and joint forces of Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA) and the Mogoke Byu Har Ah Hmat 1223 Tat Yin: Mogoke Strategy Battalion 1223 near the village from 1:50 pm to 2:10 pm and at 5:20 pm. The military forces carried out airstrikes with fighter jets from the Tada-U airbas around 4 pm and destroyed 15 houses, a clinic and a monastery. Casualties unknown.
records.actor2: PSLF/TNLA: Palaung State Liberation Front/Ta'ang National Liberation Army

records.notes: Around 19 October 2023 (as reported), in Mandalay city (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), a 30-year-old man who was arrested on 3 October by the Myanmar military troops LIB-72 station at Lawpita died at the Mandalay hospital on 19 October. The man was hospitalized after being beaten by the Myanmar military troops.

Around 18 October 2023, in in Auk Kin village (coded as Auk Kin (East)) of Tanintharyi township (Myeik district, Tanintharyi region), the military LIB-557 based in Auk Kin village detained and killed a 28-year-old man who was a driver from the Chan Myae Parami health aid charity group in their custody and informed his family on 18 October. He was arrested on 30 September at the Za Wea village checkpoint along with an ambulance and detained in the LIB-557 compound in Auk Kin village. He died of injuries sustained from torture.

On 18 October 2023, near Ye-U township (Shwebo district, Sagaing region), joint forces of the People's Defense Force - Ye-U and PDF - Shwebo District Battalion-14 carried out three mine attacks against the Myanmar military troops (LID-701, 708) and Pyu Saw Htee member who came out Ye-U Traditional Hospital. The first attack was launched at 6:30 am near Thea Taw Moe using a mine. The second attack was launched near Let Tan-1 using a mine at around 10:55 am. And, the final attack was carried out at Let Tan - 5 at around 1:20 pm. Six military soldiers died and six others were injured.

On 17 October 2023, in Myawaddy town (Myawaddy township, Myawaddy district, Kayin state), two unknown gunmen shot dead a health worker who worked for the Myawaddy District Health Department in the 4th ward, Department of Public Health District Office, Hospital compound around 5:30 pm. He was shot three times and died with critical injuries in the head. The victim was accused of being a military informant.

On 17 October 2023, in Ywar Gyi (Lower) village of Nyaungshwe township (Taunggyi district, Shan-South state), the Myanmar military forces arrested a nurse who joined the civil disobedience movement (CDM) for unknown reasons.

Property destruction: On 13 October 2023, in Kye village (Palaw township, Myeik district, Tanintharyi region), the Myanmar military column raided the village and set fire to two ambulances from a monastery.
Property destruction: On 13 October 2023, in Kway Kone village of Yesagyo township (Pakokku district, Magway region), the Myanmar military troops raided the village and destroyed a clinic.

On 11 October 2023, between Ye town and Hpa Lan village (coded as Ye) (Ye township, Mawlamyine district, Mon state), two unidentified armed men shot and seriously injured a 45-year-old man while returning to the village on a motorbike at around 2:30 pm. The victim was shot three times. No group claimed responsibility for the shooting. The victim was taken to Mawlamyine General Hospital.

records.actor: Ye Bi Lu Aphwe: Ye Ogre Group
records.notes: On 10 October 2023, in Ye town (Ye township, Mawlamyine district, Mon state), the Ye Ogre Group opened fire at a Pyu Saw Htee leader who was also a ward administrator from Aung Myitta ward near Mya Thida market at around 9 am. The man sustained injuries and was taken to Mawlamyine General Hospital. According to Khit Thit Media, the man died from injuries.

On 7 October 2023, in Salingyi township (Yinmarbin district, Sagaing region), a combined force of the Brother Defense Force - Yinmarbin and the Dark Guerrilla Force carried out a mine attack against a column of around 30 soldiers who left from Wanbao-Yangsi company compound near a 16-bedded township hospital at around 8 am. The military fired back with small weapons. Three military troops were killed, and two others sustained severe injuries.

On 6 October 2023, in Win Gyi village (coded as Kawkareik) (Kawkareik township, Kawkareik district, Kayin state), the combined troops of the Angry Bird Drone Rangers, Kloud and Power of Ababli Drone Team attacked the Myanmar military column stationed near a monastery with a 1 kg bomb attached to a drone around 3:55 pm. Three military soldiers were severely injured. A 10-year-old boy and a 46-year-old man nearby were also injured from the drone attack and they were sent to the hospital for treatment. Around 4:30 pm, another bomb was dropped by the combined force but there were no casualties the second time.

Looting: On 6 October 2023, in Hpein Kar village (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military raided the village and looted rice bags from Super Five rice mill. The military took rice bags to the Hospital of Traditional Medicine on Ye-U town where they stationed.

On 5 October 2023, in Sittwe town (Sittwe township, Sittwe district, Kahine state), an unknown armed group shot two civilians in their house around 9 pm in Sat Yone Su ward near the Arakan Liberation Army office. A 51-year-old man was shot five times and died at the hospital. His daughter was also shot and injured in the leg and arm. No group claimed responsibility for the attack. Locals claimed that the deceased was one of the leaders of the United League of Arakan.

On 5 October 2023, in Yae Shin village (coded as Ah Lel Yae Shin) (Mogaung township, Mohnyin district, Kachin state), an unknown armed group shot and killed the Yae Shin village tract administrator around 7 pm at his house. He was shot three times in the chest and hand and died on his way to the Mogaung Hospital. No group claimed responsibility for the attack. Other sources, Kachin News Group and Khit Thit Media, reported the event occurred between Ywar Thit and San Par Li villages.

On 30 September 2023, near Kyauk Mi Chaung (south) village of Ye township (coded as Kyauk Mi Kyaung) (Mawlamyine district, Mon state), an unidentified armed group fired at a light truck carrying six passengers traveling from Kyaung village to Thingangyun village with small guns at around 12 pm. A 54-year-old driver was killed on the spot and his uncle and aunt were severely injured. The uncle died later at the hospital. No one claimed responsibility for the attack. The media speculated that this event might relate to the other attack against the military vehicle (coded separately) by the Ye Orge that happened around the same time and area, suggesting the Ye Orge might be responsible for this incident.

On 25 September 2023, in Hpakant town (Hpakant township, Mohnyin district, Kachin state), an unknown armed group came to the general hospital via car at approximately 2 p.m. and fired shots at a military intelligence member and two police officers receiving medical treatment there. The three targets attacked at the hospital had been previously injured in an attack by the KIA and the PDF (under the NUG) in Hseng Taung village on 21 September 2023 (coded separately). DVB and RFA reported all three were killed. EMG reported that a colonel (military intelligence) was killed and others jumped into the U Yu stream and escaped. Network Media Group reported a colonel (military intelligence) was killed and another one who jumped into the U Yu stream drowned and died in the water. The Myanmar military at the hospital returned fire and both sides were injured. Fatalities coded as 2 using the latest reports.

records.actor: NTG: Northern Tigers Group - Sagaing Region
records.notes: On 23 September 2023, in Khin-U town of Khin-U township (Shwebo district, Sagaing region), joint forces of Northern Tiger Group, TTA: Taik Taing Aung People's Defense Force, Black Eagle Local People's Defense Force (Khin-U), PDAF: People's Defense Air Force, and Shar Htoo Waw Drone Technical Team carried out three drone strikes against the Myanmar military troops stationed at Pyu Saw Htee leader's house and near a hospital. The drone strikes killed three military soldiers.

On 23 September 2023, in Mu Thar Kone village (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military stationed at the Ye-U Traditional Medicine Hospital fired at least 20 artillery rounds in the township and some of the rounds landed in the village, hitting and killing a 17-year-old and injuring 2 other women.

On 23 September 2023, in Kyangin town (Kyangin township, Hinthada district, Ayeyarwady region), about 15 Myanmar military troops and police arrested a CDM doctor and his wife, who is a midwife, in their house at around 10 pm. The victims were accused of providing illegal medical treatment. They were then charged under Section 52(b) of Counter-Terrorism Law.

records.actor: Na Gar Min Pyi Thu Kar Kwei Yay Tat Khwe: Dragon King People's Defense Unit
records.notes: On 14 September 2023, in Na Gar Pauk village (Myaung township, Sagaing district, Sagaing region), a combined force of local resistance groups attacked about 150 military soldiers, who were attempting to set fire to a resthouse and a clinic, with three 600-gram drone bombs at around 6 am. The military shot down one of the combined force's drones with a Drone Killer. The military stationed in Kyi village also fired artillery rounds to help their ground troops. The combined force includes the Civilians Defense Security Organization Myaung (Unit - Ah Nauk Paing 001 Drone Group, Ahr Man Sit Thei and Dragon King), Taloke PDF, Sagaing District PDF (B-2 (Unit-3) and B-6 (Unit-3)), Myingyan District PDF (Unit- MYINGEN 21), Sin Yaing Column - Myaung, 11 Brothers Revolution Forces, Liberation Army of Central Region, Na Gar Pauk Area PDF, Peacock Warrior Myaung Guerrilla Force, Myaung Union Army and the Myaung Women Warriors. Casualties unknown.

records.actor: KNU/KNLA: Karen National Union/Karen National Liberation Army
records.notes: On 10 September 2023, in Taung Kyar Inn village of Kawkareik township (Kawkareik district, Kayin state), joint forces of the Karen National Liberation Army (KNLA), Karen National Defense Organization (KNDO, and allies carried out an attack against the Myanmar military troops LID-230 and LID-208 who temporarily stationed at a local health center and a school building at around 8:10 am. During the fight, the military fired artillery rounds on the battlefield, hitting and injuring a local. About 400 locals were forced to flee their homes. A military vehicle was also destroyed during the fight. Three military soldiers including a captain died and eight others were injured.

On 9 September 2023, in Ye township (Mawlamyine district, Mon state), the Myanmar military IB-61 fired artillery shells and fell into a farm. A 60-years-old farm owner fled from the artillery attack and died of respiratory distress at the Ye township hospital. Local claimed that the military fired artillery shells after the rumors that the People's Defense Force members arrived in the township.

On 5 September 2023, in Pyin Htaung Lay village (Katha township, Katha district, Sagaing region), the military soldiers stationed in Nga Oe village conducted a drone strike on the village at around 5 am. Simultaneously, about 70 military ground troops (IB-121 and LID-77) raided the village and fired over 10 artillery rounds, destroying some houses. The military then set fire to over 30 houses, including a village clinic. A number of monks and other civilians were trapped within the village. There were no casualties.

On 1 September 2023, in Zee Da Ban village of Kutkai township (Muse district, Shan-North state), fighting broke out between the Myanmar military troops and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA) near the village. A heavy artillery shells fired by the military LIB 123 killed during the clash landed in the village and killed a 37-year-old man on the spot and severely injured a 58-year-old man at around 1:30 pm. The injured man later died on the same day on the way to hospital. The shelling also injured two female civilians.
records.actor2: PSLF/TNLA: Palaung State Liberation Front/Ta'ang National Liberation Army

records.notes: On 22 August 2023, in Waw Lu village (Kawkareik township, Kawkareik district, Kayin state), the military soldiers (from the No.13 military Operation Command) arrested two locals at the intersection of Poe Yae village road and Kyaikdon Hospital road at around 5 pm and beat and hit them. The arrestees were then released at around 8 pm.

On 14 August 2023, in Mandalay - Chanayethazan (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), the Myanmar military arrested a lay attendant from Tha Mar Tha Ti monastery located near 29th and 89th streets in Pyi Gyi Pyaw Bwe (West) ward. The arrest occurred the military's propaganda media channels demanded the military to arrest him for his participation in the CDM movement. On the same day, the military arrested a female doctor on 82nd road (between 34th and 35th streets) in Aung Nan Yeik Thar (East) ward.

On 13 August 2023, in Pauk Kan village (Wetlet township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike at 3:40 pm and four civilians were injured. Two military fighter jets from Tada-U airbase fired with rockets and machine guns without the presence of the battle. A fighter jet fired south of the village and another one circled and fired the whole village. They targeted the clinic and school and locals claimed that they attacked the village because the people who were attacked by fighter jets in Ta Nei village were having treatment in Pauk Kan village. Three men and a woman were injured. Houses of the locals and the village high school was also destroyed.

Property destruction: On 12 August 2023, in Tha Khut Ta Nei village (Budalin township, Monywa district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military column of around 150 soldiers set fire to 40 houses, a clinic and shops in the village two times at 5 pm and 9 pm.

On 9 August 2023, in Mandalay - Chanmyathazi (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), the military arrested a female aid worker and the chairperson of the Shwe Mahar Nwe Social Welfare group while she was on her way to hospital on a motorcycle at noon. Before her arrest, Han Nyein Oo, the pro-military telegram channel posted her information and called for the arrest for celebrating the 35th anniversary of the 8-8-88 uprising. Her family and friends claimed that the photo posted in the telegram channel was another person and she was framed. She was arrested first time on 15 November 2021 and sentenced three years imprisonment and released on from the Obo prison early May 2023.

On 9 August 2023, in Ma Yan Inn village (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military stationed at the Ye-U Traditional Medicine Hospital fired four 120 mm artillery rounds at the village and some of the rounds hit and injured 5 civilians (3 men and 2 women), including a 13-year-old boy.

On 6 August 2023, in Kale town of Kale township (Kale district, Sagaing region), a member of the Chin National Defense Force (CNO/CNDF) arrested by the military died at the Kale General Hospital. The deceased member of CNDF was arrested by the Myanmar military troops in 2021 and sentenced to 6 years of imprisonment. The main reason for his death was unknown.

On 6 August 2023, near Nyaung Gyit Pin village of Pakokku township (Pakokku district, Magway region), joint forces of the People's Defense Force - Myaing (PaKaPha) Battalion-1 (Unit-5) and Black Cobra Force Pakokku carried out an attack using mines and small weapons against about 40 Pyu Saw Htee members and military soldiers who came out to clear mines at around 12 pm. The military troops randomly fired back using heavy and small weapons. Bodies of military soldiers and Pyu Saw Htee members killed in the attack were taken to Pakokku General Hospital by ambulance. Khit Thit Media reported that five members of Pyu Saw Htee and three military soldiers died in the attack.

On 2 August 2023, in Hpakant town (Hpakant township, Mohnyin district, Kachin state), Myanmar military troops arrived at the Kyaw Clinic in five vehicles at around 8 pm and arrested the medical doctor who is the owner of the clinic for an unknown reason. He is the former head of the Hpakant Hospital and the locals claimed that he could be arrested by the military because he employed a CDM doctor at his private clinic.

On 24 July 2023, in Shan Taw Lay village (Okpho township, Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), the KTV near Yangon-Pyay Road was attacked with a remote explosive around 10:30 am. A staff was injured in his leg and transferred to Yangon General Hospital from Okpho Township hospital. The Underground Guerrilla Forces - Okpo claimed they attacked the KTV due to its association with the military.

On 22 July 2023, in Khin-U town (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the combined force of Mandalay People Rescue (MPR) and Armed Revolutionary Force attacked the Myanmar military and Pyu Saw Htee troops stationed in Khin-U hospital at 9 am with small arms. 2 soldiers were killed during the attack and their bodies were carried into the hospital. The military returned fire.
