On 20 July 2023, in Mu Thar village (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military stationed at the Ye-U Traditional Medicine Hospital fired 120 mm artillery rounds which landed in the village in the morning. Two villagers were killed and three others sustained severe injuries.

On 19 July 2023, in Thet Pay village (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military stationed at the traditional medicine hospital in Ye-U town fired rounds of 120mm mortar into the village at around 11:45 am. A six-year-old child was killed, and five other family members including a 47-year-old man sustained injuries. Myanmar Now and the RFA reported that the injured man died while receiving medical treatment. Other sources, VOA and Radio Free Asia reported that 3 locals were killed. Fatalities coded as 3 using the latest report.

On 18 July 2023, near Ye-U town (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military stationed at the Ye-U Traditional Medicine Hospital fired artillery rounds and some of the rounds landed near the town. Casualties unknown.

On 17 July 2023, between Inn Pat and Ywar Thit Kone villages (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military stationed at the Ye-U Traditional Medicine Hospital fired 81 mm artillery rounds and four of the rounds landed between the villages. Casualties unknown.

On 16 July 2023, in Inn Pat village (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military stationed at the Ye-U Traditional Medicine Hospital fired two rounds of 60mm mortar into the village. The mortar shells hit a school where IDPs were staying, killing a 50-year-old male IDP and injuring another 30-year-old male IDP. Three cattle belonging to IDPs were also killed by the shells. Myanmar Now reported that those killed and injured are a 48-year-old man and a 40-year-old man.

On 15 July 2023, in Pa Leik (Ywar Ma) village of Sintgaing township (Kyaukse district, Mandalay region), two alleged members of the People's Defense Force - Myinge shot dead a female medical doctor who was working at the Myanmar railway hospital for unknown reasons.

On 8 July 2023, in Mandalay - Chanayethazan (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), military soldiers in plain clothes arrested the leader of the Demon Underground Revolutionary Force outside a clinic. The arrest took place in the morning while he was going to the clinic to seek medical treatment for an illness in the Hay Mar Za La ward. A woman was also arrested along with him. Based on Myanmar Now, it could not be confirmed if the woman detained with him was also a member of the group.
Property destruction: On 7 July 2023, in Ma Gyi Kan village (Myinmu township, Sagaing district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military set fire to houses and a clinic.

On 2 July 2023, in Hakha town (Hakha township, Hakha district, Chin state), the local Chin resistance groups attacked the military soldiers stationed at the general hospital at around 9 pm and both sides exchanged fire. Casualties unknown.

On 1 July 2023, in Ann township (Kyaukpyu district, Rakhine state), a 29-year-old man who was arrested by military forces at a bus station in Thandwe in October 2021 died while receiving medical treatment at the Ann people's hospital during the military's interrogation. His family members said that he died because he was not provided medical treatment in time.

On 28 June 2023, in Thantlang town of Thantlang township (Hakha district, Chin state), the Myanmar military forces carried out three airstrikes and destroyed a general hospital and two houses at around 12 pm. Mizzima reported that the airstrikes targeted the Chin National Army (CNA). There were no casualties.
records.actor2: CNF/CNA: Chin National Front/Chin National Army

records.actor: People's Defense Force - Myeik District Battalion 2
records.notes: On 17 June 2023, between Tha Mee Hla and Ban Law villages (coded as Tha Mee Hla) (Tanintharyi township, Myeik district, Tanintharyi region), a combined force of the People's Defense Force - Myeik District Battalion 2, the Kaw Thoo Lei Army, the Impartial Guerrilla Group Tanintharyi Township, the Southern Warriors Defense Force, the Peacock Special Column, the Myeik District PDF (PaKaPha), and the Taw Pone Gyi People's Defense Force attacked a Myanmar military column of around 80 soldier from the Tanintharyi based LIB 561 and the Thein Daw camp at around 10 am. Both sides then engaged in fighting and the fighting lasted until 5 pm. During the clash, the military fired heavy weapons, injuring two people and damaging houses. Many villagers from the Nyaung Pin Kwin, Chauk Maing, Sin Khaung, Ban Law, Ban Lin, Tha Mee Hla, Kaw Taw, Yae Paw, Ban Kyoke, Tha Kyet and Kywe Htein Kone villages were also forced to flee. In the statement released by KTL army, the combined force claimed that at least 30 soldiers were killed and one of their members was injured. Another source, Network Media Group, reported that five military soldiers including a high rank military doctor died and ten others were injured citing the statement of the Impartial Guerrilla Group. Fatalities coded 5 using the lowest estimate.

On 7 June 2023, in Kyauktaga town (Kyauktaga township, Bago district, Bago-East region), the Bago Region PDF opened fire on a lieutenant commander (also a member of the Kyauktaga Township State Administration Council) and his follower (also an administrator of the Baw Hti Kwin village) while they were traveling together on a motorcycle (assumed to be off-duty) at about 1 pm. Both commander and the administrator died upon arrival at a hospital.

On 7 June 2023, in Mandalay - Aungmyaythazan (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), the police beat and arrested a female doctor on the 86th street between 12th and 14th streets at about 9:47 pm for unknown reasons.

On 1 June 2023, in Yan Bo (Lower) village of Mabein township (Mongmit district, Shan-North state), the Myanmar military forces carried out airstrikes on a school building and a local clinic at around 10:30 pm. The airstrikes destroyed the school building and the clinic and killed about 6 people, according to Khit Thit Media. The airstrikes came without any armed clash in the area. Another news, Shwe Phee Myay News Agency reported that there were 4 civilian injuries. Fatalities coded as 0 for the lowest and most up-to-date estimates.

Property destruction: On 29 May 2023, in Hlut Taik village (Kyunhla township, Kanbalu district, Sagaing region), around 170 military soldiers from the LIB 361 and Pyu Saw Htee set fire to seven houses and a rural clinic.
Property destruction: On 27 May 2023, in Nwe Ni village (Yesagyo township, Pakokku district, Magway region), the military set fire to 26 houses and a clinic.

On 22 May 2023, in Hpakant town (Hpakant township, Mohnyin district, Kachin state), an unknown armed group shot and injured a 40-year-old man in front of the Doh Nyi Naung betal shop in Hmaw Wan ward around 2 am. He was sent to the Hpakant hospital. The reasons for the attack and the culprits were unknown.

On 21 May 2023, in Hakha town (Hakha township, Hakha district, Chin state), military forces raided the Agape hospital at night and arrested a female doctor and four female nurses for unknown reasons. The arrestees were detained at the Hakha police station. The doctor were released three days later while four nurses were also released in the second week of June 2023. On the same day, six youths were also arrested in the Pyi Taw Thar ward.

On 17 May 2023, in Saw Lon village (Bawlake township, Bawlake district, Kayah state), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike and dropped bombs around 4 am without an armed clash occurring. A 17-year-old girl was killed and four elderly men were injured. Six houses and two clinics were destroyed.

On 17 May 2023, in Saw Lon village (Bawlake township, Bawlake district, Kayah state), the military conducted airstrikes two times at around 4 am, destroying 6 residential houses, two clinics, and a monastery. The airstrikes carried out by the military also killed a civilian woman and injured 4 men.

On 9 May 2023, in Taung Sa Lone village (Pinlaung township, Pa-O Self-Administered Zone, Shan-South state), the military conducted an airstrike by dropping two bombs on the village at around 11 pm, destroying two clinics and eight residential houses. Casualties unknown.

records.location: Ah Lu Ah Nauk Ywar Gyi
records.notes: On 6 May 2023, in Ah Lu Ah Nauk Ywar village of Bilin township (coded as Ah Lu Ah Nauk Ywar Gyi) (Thaton district, Mon state), the Myanmar military troops stationed on Alone Taung fired heavy artillery shells and injured two local villagers, a husband and wife at around 9 pm. The two victims were taken to Thaton Hospital. Three houses in the village were destroyed and locals were forced to flee.

On 2 May 2023, in Hle Lan Ku village (Htantabin township, Taungoo district, Bago-East region), following the attack on a military base in the Bon Ma Tee village by the KNLA at around 10 am (event coded separately), the military forces carried out an airstrike on the village at around 6 pm, destroying the Hle Lan Ku station hospital. Casualties unknown.

On 1 May 2023, in Thandaung town (Thandaunggyi township, Hpa-An district, Kayin state), a young man wearing a boonie hat and riding a motorcycle was arrested by patrolling military soldiers on the Thandaung Hospital Road in the morning.

On 20 April 2023, in Madaya town (Madaya township, Pyinoolwin district, Mandalay region), a combined force of the Unicorn Guerrilla Force and the Sein Pan Ni Ni Drone Aphwe: Red Flamboyant Flower Drone Team attacked the military soldiers stationed in the police station and the administration office with three bombs attached to three drones at around noon, two of the bombs landed on the police station and the administration office and another bomb landed on the hospital nearby. The military also fired back with small guns. Casualties unknown.

On 19 April 2023, in Ma Gyi Kan village (Myaing township, Pakokku district, Magway region), the military arrested a doctor, and a teacher for unknown reasons. The military also detained 14 locals to serve as porters. Irrawaddy reported that 19 locals were detained as porters.

On 13 April 2023, in Zee Chaing village of Ngapudaw township (Pathein district, Ayeyarwady region), a combined force of the Pathein Western Defense Force, the Beach Defense Force, the SinYine Guerrilla Force, the West Coast Defense Force and Yoma Annawah Defense Force opened fire at a 48-year-old man, who is known to be a Myanmar military informant, with five gunshots in his house at around 8:30 pm. The victim was critically injured and taken to the military hospital. The attack was carried out as part of Operation Nan Htike Aung (under the NUG).

On 11 April 2023, in Inn Pat village (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military stationed in the Ye-U traditional medicine hospital fired 60 mm artillery rounds and two of which landed on a school in the village, injuring two IDPs inside. Network Media Group reported that two other locals were also injured.

On 11 April 2023, between Inn Pat and Kone Thar villages (coded as Inn Pat) (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military stationed in the Ye-U traditional medicine hospital fired 120 mm artillery rounds and two of which landed on a resthouse, injuring two IDPs.

On 8 April 2023, in Ta Laing village of Sagaing township (Sagaing district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military forces carried out airstrikes on a clinic in the village using MI35 at around 11:15 am. Following the airstrikes, about 20 military troops were airdropped in the village with MI17 helicopter. VOA reported that two civilians were injured in the airstrikes. Khit Thit Media reported that a 60-year-old man was injured.

On 5 April 2023, in Si Thar village (Shwegu township, Bhamo district, Kachin state), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes three times between 4 pm and 5 pm, following an armed clash between the military and an unidentified armed group in the morning. The military LIB-10, LIB-77 and LIB-88 under the LID-88 also fired 82mm artillery rounds into the village. Around 100 houses including a village clinic were burned down by airstrikes. There were no casualties.

On 3 April 2023, in Mandalay - Pyigyitagon (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), four members of the Aung Si Taw - Mandalay opened fire on a 42-year-old military appointed administrator while he was with his 26-year-old nephew in front of the London clinic he owned at the corner of the 131st street and 53rd street in the (Sa Lain) ward. The nephew fought back with a knife. The administrator was killed on the spot. The nephew was severely injured.

On 28 March 2023, at the exit of Shwebo town (Shwebo township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), two mine explosions occurred at the highway bus station and on the Mu bridge at around 9:30 am. A four-year-old child and a 60-year-old man from Taung Twin village were killed and at least 20 people were injured. The victims were taken to the Shwebo general hospital and six victims with critical injuries were transferred to the Mandalay general hospital. The Myanmar military claimed that the attacks were carried out by the PDF and 19 people including 11 men and eight women were injured. The PDF claimed that the military and the Pyu Saw Htee were responsible for the explosions. No group claimed responsibility.

On 28 March 2023, in Ywangan town (Ywangan township, Danu Self-Administered Zone, Shan-South state), a villager from Kyauk Ku Pyin, who was arrested by the Myanmar military along with eight other men, died at the Ywangan general hospital after sustaining injuries during interrogation.

On 25 March 2023, in Kan Ma Laing village (Tanintharyi township, Myeik district, Tanintharyi region), the Myanmar military soldiers shot two brothers on a motorcycle for not stopping their motorcycle while passing through the military checkpoint of the Kan Ma Laing village at around 1 pm. A 18-year-old younger brother was killed on the spot and another died while receiving treatment at the clinic at about 2 pm. The military also arrested another two men on a motorcycle at night. Network Media Group reported that they stopped at the checkpoint but got shot after driving away when a soldier accused them of being PDF members.

Property destruction: On 8 March 2023, in Nyaung Pin Gyi village (Shwegyin township, Bago district, Bago-East region), the Myanmar military set fire to three motorcycles and medical equipment from a clinic operated by CDM health workers.

On 25 February 2023, in Lashio town (Lashio township, Lashio district, Shan-North state), police forces from the No (2) police station arrested four CDM government staff, including a nurse and a teacher, at around 3:30 pm and took them to the No (2) police station. The CDM nurse and the teacher were sentenced to two years in prison and hard labor on 23 March.

Around 23 February 2023 (between 21 - 25 February), outside of Kyun Pin village (Katha township, Katha district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military killed a 40-year-old nurse from Moe Dar Gyi village who was arrested on 21 February. The body of the victim was found on 25 February with her hands being tied behind her back and there was a gunshot in her forehead.

On 21 February 2023, in Moe Dar Gyi village (Katha township, Katha district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military arrested a 40-year-old nurse who had joined the civil disobedience movement (CDM) and opened a gas station in the village. She was arrested at her gas station with the accusation of supporting the PDF and providing healthcare to CDM staff and PDF members.

Property destruction: On 19 February 2023, in Nwe Inn village (Pale township, Yinmarbin district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military and the Pyu Saw Htee set fire to the huts of IDPs and a rural health clinic.

On 16 February 2023, near Kyauk Hpyu Taung village of Okpho township (Thayet district, Bago-West region), the Myanmar military troops shot dead a nurse who joined civil disobedience movement (CDM) for refusing to provide location of PDF camp. She was arrested by the Myanmar military forces on 29 January while traveling to Okpho town along with two members of PDF. She was blindfolded and shot dead by the Myanmar military troops on 16 February near Kyauk Hpyu Taung village. She was raped before being killed.

On 4 February 2023, in Yesagyo township (Pakokku district, Magway region), the Yesagyo PDF attacked about 14 military soldiers and the Pyu Saw Htee on two cars in front of a gas station on the Pakokku - Yesagyo road at around 4 pm and both sides then exchanged fire. During the fight, the Yesagyo PDF seized six Carbine guns, an MA-4 gun, six Carbine cartridges, five MA-4 cartridges, 240 pieces of bullets, three grenades, three 40 mm artillery rounds, and other equipment. The military also set fire to 5 houses nearby during the fight. There were 7 fatalities and six injuries on the military and Pyu Saw Htee side. DVB and Khit Thit Media further reported that three injured military soldiers died from injuries in the hospital. A total of fatalities coded as 10.

On 3 February 2023, in the east side of Loikaw town (Loikaw township, Loikaw district, Kayah state), the military carried out three airstrikes on the Elephant IDP camp at around 6 pm. 4,422 people in the camp were forced to flee. The schools, medical store, clinic, almost 200 rice sacks in the camp were destroyed. There were no casualties.

On 2 February 2023, in Pale town of Pale township (Yinmarbin district, Sagaing region), the Black Peacock Guerrilla carried out mine attack against the Myanmar military troops near Pale Township Hospital at around 9 am. The attack killed three military soldiers and injured eight others.

On 2 February 2023, in Pu Htoe village tract (Magway township, Magway district, Magway region), the military arrested two brothers who came back from a clinic at around 2 am for unknown reasons. The military also arrested the motorcycle driver who carried them and a motorcycle parking guard.
On 23 January 2023, in Aye Mya Thayar village of Hpakant township (Mohnyin district, Kachin state), the Myanmar military forces arrested a medical doctor at around 10 am for unknown reasons.

On 22 January 2023, in Monywa town (Monywa township, Monywa district, Sagaing region), around 15 Myanmar military soldiers raided the Shwe Taung Myittar philanthropic clinic located at the Pyi Lone Chan Thar monastery to search for CDM doctors and nurses. The military then arrested the abbot of the monastery and destroyed and seized the equipment of the clinic. The whereabouts of the abbot were unknown. Also, the Myanmar military raided the Shwe Taw Win private hospital and arrested the head of the hospital and two health workers.

On 21 January 2023, in Kyein Chaung village of Pyaung Ma Htein village tract (Kyainseikgyi township, Kawkareik district, Kayin state), the Myanmar military launched an airstrike and dropped around six bombs on No.2 tactical command headquarters of the DKBA with three fighter jets at around 1 am. A house of the DKBA commander was targeted and destroyed by the airstrike, but he and his family escaped. A village clinic and some staff buildings of the DKBA were damaged. Two drivers of the DKBA sustained minor injuries.

On 21 January 2023, in Ba Hin village (Myaing township, Pakokku district, Magway region), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes on the Ba Hin station hospital, which was used as a clinic by the PDF under the NUG, with a Mi-35 helicopter gunship at around 1 pm without an armed clash occurring, suspecting that there were PDF members and CDM doctors in the hospital. A 60-year-old woman, who was hiding under a Tamarind tree near the hospital, was killed by an airstrike. The roof of the hospital and the X-ray room were damaged. Around 50 patients were evacuated from the hospital.

On 10 January 2023, in Mandalay - Mahaaungmyay (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), military forces arrested the owner of 'Kyel Sin Lin' hospital on 60th street between 35th and 36th streets in the afternoon for allegedly employing the CDM doctors.

On 10 January 2023, in Mandalay - Chanayethazan (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), military forces arrested the owner of the 'Du Wun' hospital on the 28th street between 73rd and 74th streets at around 5 pm. The reason for the arrest was unknown.

On 7 January 2023, in Hpapun township (Hpapun district, Kayin state), the military conducted airstrikes to help their group troops during the battle with the KNU/KNLA combined force in Me Waing village, and some of these bombs landed on four other areas. The military conducted airstrikes with seven aircraft from around 7:40 am to 9 am and 20 bombs from these aircraft landed in the Day Bu Noh area, destroying the farms belonging to the villagers. Another four bombs then landed again in the Day Bu Noh area at around 10:10 am, injuring a villager and destroying a house and a car. Some bombs from the military's airstrikes also landed in Me Thu village tract at around 10 am, destroying a hospital and seven houses in the Le Htoo Hpoe area, and killing 6 gold mine workers in Yar Gi Htar village.

On 4 January 2023, in Myeik town of Myeik township (Myeik district, Tanintharyi region), the Myanmar military forces rearrested an assistant medical doctor who was released along with 20 others political prisoners in Independence Day Amnesty.

On 26 December 2022, in Kale town (Kale township, Kale district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military forces arrested a medical doctor who joined the civil disobedience movement (CDM) in Myothar Ward.

On 26 December 2022, in Yae Yin village of Thabeikkyin township (Pyinoolwin district, Mandalay region), joint forces of Myinmu Lightning Guerrilla Force and People's Defense Force - Hpone Kone carried out an attack using machete against a member of Pyu Saw Htee and a 100 Household Head. The member of Pyu Saw Htee died of wound sustained from the attack at Ta Kaung hospital.
On 22 December 2022, in Shwe Baung village (Madaya township, Pyinoolwin district, Mandalay region), military forces beat and arrested a CDM nurse at around 4 pm.

On 17 December 2022, in Gar Ga Law village of Kanbalu township (Kanbalu district, Sagaing region), about 120 Myanmar military soldiers from 368th Light Infantry Division (LIB) and Pyu Saw Htee members fired artillery rounds and raided the village and detained five villagers, including two women. Villagers later found three male dead bodies. Whereabouts of two women are still unknown. They also set fire to a monastery and a local clinic.

Property destruction: On 17 December 2022, in Kanbalu township (Kanbalu district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military and the Pyu Saw Htee set fire to a monastery, a clinic and 23 houses in Gar Ga Law village.

On 11 December 2022, in Taung Kyar Inn village (Kawkareik township, Kawkareik district, Kayin state), the military stationed in Tan Dar Kyoe village (under the No.12 Military Command Headquarters) fired 120 mm artillery rounds at around 7:05 pm and five of which landed behind the village hospital, destroying two houses. There were no casualties.

On 9 December 2022, near Lawt Sar village of Man Wein village tract (coded as Man Wein) (Waingmaw township, Myitkyina district, Kachin state), an unidentified armed group opened fire on a ten-wheel truck carrying military soldiers and gold-digging workers in the evening, injuring a 50-year-old driver and a woman on the vehicle. The driver later died from injuries in the Waingmaw hospital.
Property destruction: On 2 December 2022, in Ma Au village (Shwebo township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military set fire to over 30 houses and a clinic.

On 1 December 2022, in Kaing Taw Ma village (Myaing township, Pakokku district, Magway region), a combined force of the Myaing PDF (PaKaPha), God of Burmese Army, and the Galon Guerrilla Force - Myaing attacked 10 military soldiers near a hospital with two mines at around 7 am, killing 2 and injuring 2 military soldiers.

On 24 November 2022, in Par Laung village of Loikaw township (Loikaw district, Kayah state), the Karenni Nationalities Defense Force (KNDF) Battalion-2 attacked 100 military troops stationed at a clinic and a monastery and both sides exchanged fire for the entire day. During the fight, the military shot dead a civilian who traveled from Hsu Hpe Law with a motorbike. There were 10 fatalities on the military side.

On 20 November 2022, in Yangon - Hlaing (Yangon-West district, Yangon region), the military arrested a female employee from a clinic and money transfer shop in No.4 ward, accusing her of transferring money to the PDFs.

On 20 November 2022, in Launglon town (Launglon township, Dawei district, Tanintharyi region), the military, who were checking the motorcycles near the general hospital, opened fire at two locals on a motorcycle, hitting and killing one of them while the military beat and arrested the another. The gunshots of the military also hit two siblings on another motorcycle nearby, killing the brother and injuring the sister. A total of 2 fatalities were coded in this event.

On 20 November 2022, in Patheingyi township (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), the military chased down four members of the Generation Z Power MDY who were returning from Patheingyi by motorcycles and opened fire at them near the Hospital for Respiratory Disease on the 19th street at around 1:30 pm. The two sides then exchanged fire. All four members were killed and the gunshots of the military also hit and killed a local on a motorcycle nearby. The military seized the motorcycle of the local. Total fatalities coded as 5.

On 19 November 2022, in Palaw town (Palaw township, Myeik district, Tanintharyi region), 10 military soldiers and the police arrested a patient from the General Hospital, who was injured and hospitalized by the military drone attack in Ka De village on 14 November.
On 18 November 2022, in Yangon - Mayangone (Yangon-West district, Yangon region), the military arrested three locals in front of the Victoria hospital for unknown reasons at around 3:45 pm.

Property destruction: On 16 November 2022, in Sat Thwar village of Yesagyo township (Pakokku district, Magway region), the Myanmar military troops raided the village and set fire to six houses, a residence of civil servants, and a clinic.

records.actor: Thayarwady Galon Tatphwe: Thayarwady Galon Force
records.notes: On 14 November 2022, in Thayarwady township (Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), Thayarwady Galon Force shot two military-appointed administrators of the Aik Ka Don village tract and Myit Kyoe village tract at 6 pm. The administrator of the Aik Ka Don village tract was killed on the spot and the administrator of the Myit Kyoe village tract was seriously injured and taken to the Thayarwady hospital. The attack was carried out under the name of Operation Nan Htike Aung (under the NUG).

On 14 November 2022, in Myo Kone village (Shwegu township, Bhamo district, Kachin state), the military fired heavy weapons and the shells landed in the Myo Kone hospital. The hospital was damaged. There were no casualties.
On 14 November 2022, in Mandalay city (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), a CDM nurse who was arrested by the military on 29 October 2022 was killed during the military's interrogation.

On 6 November 2022, in Sittwe town (Sittwe township, Sittwe district, Pathein region), the Myanmar military arrested a man, who is in charge of the Arakan Blood Donor (ABD) Association, when he came to the Sittwe hospital to check on the condition of a director of the Department of Fisheries who was shot. Some unknown numbers of locals were also detained after the attack.

records.actor: Police Forces of Myanmar (2021-) Border Guard Police
records.notes: On 1 November 2022, in Buthidaung town of Buthidaung township (Maungdaw district, Rakhine state), the Say Oe Kya village administrator who is a Rohingya died at Buthidaung Hospital after being arrested for the past six months. The victim family claims that he was murdered because they were not informed of any worsening illness or medical condition of him before Border Guard Police took him from the hospital. The family paid 10 million MMK ($4760) to authorities for his release.

On 31 October 2022, in Mandalay - Aungmyaythazan (Mandalay District, Mandalay Region), the Myanmar military troops arrested nine medical doctors who joined the civilian disobedience movement (CDM) for unknown reasons.

On 31 October 2022, in Nant Thar Myaing village of Madaya township (Pyinoolwin District, Mandalay Region), about 20 Myanmar military troops raided Yae Nant Thar Leprosy Hospital and arrested seven civilians at around 11:30 am for unknown reasons.

On 24 October 2022, in Poke Ba Thi Ri township (Oke Ta Ra district, Nay Pyi Taw), the Myanmar military arrested a military major, who was serving as a military doctor at the 300-bed Nay Pyi Taw Hospital, for changing his Facebook profile to black as a gesture of mourning for the victims of the military's airstrikes in Hpakant township in Kachin state. He was arrested at the hospital and taken to an interrogation camp at the Nay Pyi Taw Prison.

On 22 October 2022, in Kya Khat Ta Pin village (Yebyu township, Dawei district, Tanintharyi region), an unknown armed group opened fire on five people including the female middle school principal, her husband (a retired teacher) and a married couple at their homes. Four out of five people (the school principal, her husband and the married couple) were shot and killed. The other one was injured and receiving treatment at the Yebyu hospital.

On 21 October 2022, in Kyaung Lel village (Banmauk township, Katha district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military conducted an airstrike on a hospital operated by the National Unity Government and staff housing at around 1 pm, destroying medical equipment, three buildings and killing a local and 2 cows.

On 20 October 2022, in Man Yu Gyi village of Banmauk township (Katha district, Sagaing region), the military carried out airstrikes on a local clinic during its opening ceremony at around 4:20 pm. The airstrikes killed a 40-year-old woman and injured some locals, including five children. The airstrikes also destroyed the hospital and five residential houses.

On 14 October 2022, in Letpadan town (Letpadan township, Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), a man (the owner of musical instrument and spectacles shop), who was arrested by the military and police on 7 September, was tortured during detention and died at the Thayarwady hospital from injuries and his underlying disease. The police informed his family that the victim died of diabetes but the family claimed that he was a healthy man with no diabetes.

On 7 October 2022, in Mun Daunt Chaung Hte village (Paletwa township, Matupi district, Chin state), the Myanmar military fired artillery rounds at the village at around 5:30 pm without an armed clash occurring. A 57-year-old woman was injured by an artillery shell and she was taken to the Paletwa general hospital.

On 30 September 2022, in Kyauk Khwet village of Myaing township (Pakokku district, Magway region), a 5-year-old child stepped on a landmine planted by the Myanmar military troops in April and exploded at around 12 pm. The child lost two of his legs. He later died at the hospital. Khit Thit Media later reported that the kid died at 3 pm.

On 29 September 2022, near Kyauk Mat Tat and Kyauk Ni Maw villages (coded as Kyauk Mat Tat) (Launglon township, Dawei district, Tanintharyi region), the Launglon PDF attacked 5 members of the Pyu Saw Htee, who returned from Kyauk Mat Tat village, at around 12:35 pm. The two sides exchanged fire for around an hour. Two Pyu Saw Htee members died on the spot and another two injured members died at the hospital on the same day. The group also seized a firearm, four magazines, one 9mm pistol and ammunition.

On 27 September 2022, in Thea Taw village (Kawlin township, Kawlin district, Sagaing region), fighting broke out between the Myanmar military and unidentified local defense forces after the military raided the village. Two villagers, who tried to flee the clash, stepped on landmines planted by local defense forces. One of them died at the Mandalay Hospital and another one lost his leg.

On 27 September 2022, in Sittwe town (Sittwe township, Sittwe district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military arrested a doctor from Nga Pa Thon village of Myebon township after the military found that the medicines transported by a boat from Sittwe to the village belonged to him. The military detained him after he was summoned to Sittwe town. Also, the Myanmar military arrested the Minbya township deputy education officer after he was summoned to the Rakhine state education office. He was accused of deducting the salaries of teachers to support the Arakan Army for over two years. He was charged under Section 17(1) of the Unlawful Associations Act.

On 25 September 2022, in Mandalay - Chanmyathazi (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), policemen in plain clothes arrested a doctor, the owner of the Aung Clinic at the corner of 65th and 118 streets at about 8 pm.

On 25 September 2022, in Pauktaw township (Sittwe district, Rakhine state), the Myanmar military forces arrested a medical doctor from Nga Pa Thone village in Myebon township while transporting medicine to a local hospital.

On 14 September 2022, near Kyun Chaung village (coded as Kyun Chaung Lay) (Pakokku township, Pakokku district, Magway region), the Pakokku PDF carried out mine attacks against Pyu Saw Htee members, who were traveling in six motorcycles and a car, near the Chaung Ma Gyi creek. A motorcycle ridden by three Pyu Saw Htee members was hit by a mine and one of them died on the spot. Another Pyu Saw Htee member died at the hospital and another one sustained critical injuries.

On 11 September 2022, in Hpa-An town (Hpa-An township, Hpa-An district, Kayin state), the Hpa-An Guerrilla Force attacked the military stationed near a hospital with bombs at around 7 am, destroying a military vehicle. Casualties unknown.

On 8 September 2022, in Sittwe town (Sittwe township, Sittwe district, Rakhine state), a police inspector from Sittwe No. 1 Police Station (assumed to be off-duty) was shot dead by an unknown gunman in front of the mortuary near the Sittwe town public hospital while he was waiting on a motorcycle along with his wife and daughter to cross the traffic light at around 6:30 pm. His wife and daughter were not injured.

On 8 September 2022, near Pein Hne Taw village (Thanbyuzayat township, Mawlamyine district, Mon state), an ambulance of the Aung Mingalar Funeral and Social Relief Association returning from sending a patient who had a motorcycle accident in Ye town to Mawlamyine hospital was attacked with mines and guns at around 1 am. The driver and his follower from the Association were injured. The ambulance was damaged. No group claimed responsibility for the act.

On 6 September 2022, in Taunggyi town of Taunggyi township (Taunggyi district, Shan-South state), a 43-year-old leader of Southern Shan State People's Defense Force who was arrested on 5 August died in Sao San Tun Hospital at around 3:30 am after being tortured during military interrogation.

On 29 August 2022, between Ta Rein and Rakhine villages of Maungdaw township (coded as Maungdaw) (Maungdaw district, Rakhine state), a 29-year-old health worker from an INGO named the Malteser Inter was hit by landmines and injured from explosion on his way to banana field at around 6:30 pm.

On 29 August 2022, in Shwe Chaung village of Yesagyo township (Pakokku district, Magway region), the Myanmar military fired artillery rounds indiscriminately from the Pauk Taw Gyi river port to the eastern part of the Chindwin river. A 17-year-old girl from Shwe Chaung village, who was working on a motorboat in the Chindwin river, was killed by artillery shells. She died on the way to the hospital.

On 23 August 2022, in Mu Thar Kone village (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), military forces from the Ye-U town traditional hospital fired rounds of heavy artillery at the village at around 8:30 pm and a shell hit and injured a 7-year-old child, a 30-year-old man and a 30-year-old woman.

On 23 August 2022, in Ywar Thar Lay village (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), military forces from the Ye-U town traditional hospital fired rounds of heavy artillery at the village at around 8:30 pm. Casualties unknown.

On 23 August 2022, in Koke Ko Su village (Ye-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), military forces from the Ye-U town traditional hospital fired rounds of heavy artillery at the village at around 8:30 pm. Casualties unknown.

On 23 August 2022, in Inn Pat village (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), military forces from the Ye-U town traditional hospital fired rounds of heavy artillery at the village at around 8:30 pm. Casualties unknown.

On 23 August 2022, in Mu Thar village (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), military forces from the Ye-U town traditional hospital fired rounds of heavy artillery at the village at around 8:30 pm and the shells hit and injured a 40-year-old man and a cow in the village.

Property destruction: On 21 August 2022, in Hnan Khar village (Gangaw township, Gangaw district, Magway region), a military column burned down an IDP camp, a funeral car and a clinic that treated refugees at around 4 pm.

On 11 August 2022, in Ye town (Ye township, Mawlamyine district, Mon state), the military forces arrested a nurse who participated in the Civil Disobedience Movement at her home on Aung Mingalar 2 street at around 7:30 pm, accusing her of being involved with people's defense forces.

Property destruction: On 10 August 2022, in Nga Ta Yaw village (Yesagyo township, Pakokku district, Magway region), the military forces and Pyu Saw Htee opened fire and raided the village. They then destroyed houses, a monastery and a clinic at around 10 am. They also looted gold, money, rice oil and food products and motorbikes.

On 9 August 2022, in Daw Par Pa village of Loikaw township (Loikaw district, Kayah state), the Myanmar military's airstrikes hit a clinic at around 10 am and killed a 70-year-old local and injured two others. The clinic and houses were damaged. Villagers and displaced people taking refuge in the village were forced to flee their homes. The military's No (250) Artillery Battalion from Loikaw town also fired artillery shells at the village.

On 7 August 2022, in Yangon - Shwepyithar (Yangon-North district, Yangon region), the military arrested a vet, a doctor and a family member from a house across the Myint Mo private school on the Hlaw Kar road at around 8 pm for unknown reasons.

On 6 August 2022, in Taunggyi town (Taunggyi township, Taunggyi district, Shan-South state), the military arrested a woman employee from a private hospital (Soe San Tun) housing at around 4 pm after a pro-military social media accused her of supporting the PDFs in the morning.

On 26 July 2022, in Ma Gyi Oke village (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), a 13-year-old boy was injured in his chest and arm when 80 military soldiers opened fire on the residential areas in the village at around 5 am. And a leader of a local defense force (unarmed) who went to save the injured boy was shot and killed by the military while he was driving a car to the nearest clinic for medical treatment along with the boy's mother and another villager after encountering the military soldiers. The boy's mother was beaten. Another villager in the car escaped. The injured boy bled to death while he was being evacuated and going to a nearby clinic for treatment. The military seized the car, cash and cell phones from the victims.

On 25 July 2022, in Kyauk Yae Twin village (Bilin township, Thaton district, Mon state), two unknown gunmen shot an administrator of the Kyauk Yae Twin village while he was sitting at a tea shop at about 7 am. He was shot in the abdomen and area above the pubic bone and died while receiving medical treatment at the Mawlamyine Public Hospital.

On 24 July 2022, in Yangon - Dagon Myothit-North (Yangon-East district, Yangon region), the Myanmar military raided a house on No.1 Mya Thiri street in No.30 ward at around 3:30 pm. The Eleven Media Group reported that there was an exchange of fire between the military and young people, and one of them was killed and five were captured. It reported that the military also seized two pistols, four magazines, 239 bullets of 9mm firearm, five homemade bombs and two homemade grenades. However, Khit Thit Media reported that the Myanmar military opened fire at young people who were living in the house with around 40 gunshots and there was no exchange of fire. Two young people were killed and two were arrested. DVB news referring to a witness later reported that there was an exchange of fire and two young people were killed and transported by ambulances. Three others were also captured. Fatalities coded as 2 using the most recent estimate.
Property destruction: On 23 July 2022, in Ngar Tin Gyi village (Khin-U township, Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military raided the village and set a village clinic on fire.

On 4 July 2022, in Hpapun district (Kayin state), the Myanmar military launched airstrikes with two fighter jets against a base of the Arakan Army located in an area controlled by the KNU Brigade 5 near the Thai border at 12:25 pm. There was no fighting between the two sides and the base is located away from the ongoing fighting between the military and the KNU. 6 members of the Arakan Army were killed and several injured. The airstrikes also damaged some buildings including a hospital and a garment factory.
records.actor2: ULA/AA: United League of Arakan/Arakan Army

records.notes: Property destruction: On 27 June 2022, in See Maw village (Banmauk township, Katha district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military destroyed and torched a clinic of a CDM health worker.

On 26 June 2022, in Kawlin town (Kawlin township, Kawlin district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military arrested a woman from a rice mill in Thuwanna ward and a female doctor from Zay Yon Bo Te ward. The reason for the arrest was not reported.
On 18 June 2022, in Mandalay city (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), military forces arrested a CDM doctor at his home for an unknown reason.

On 16 June 2022, in Way Di village (Launglon township, Dawei district, Tanintharyi region), military forces entered the village by opening fire and abducted a 30-year-old woman at her home and shot her at a closer range on one side of the road. She was seriously injured and being treated at the Dawei General hospital. The military also set a house on fire.
Property destruction: On 16 June 2022, in Maung Htaung village (Budalin township, Monywa district, Sagaing region), military forces set five houses on fire and established their camp in the hospital.

On 16 June 2022, in Maung Htaung village (Budalin township, Monywa district, Sagaing region), in the morning, the military forces raided the village, abducted 60 civilians from the village, and held them as hostages in the hospital building.

records.actor: PNO/PNA: Pa-Oh National Organization/Pa-Oh National Army
records.notes: On 14 June 2022, in Hsaik Hkawng Hti Tat village of Hsihseng township (Pa-O Self-Administered Zone, Shan-South state), a 22-year-old woman who was shot by the Pa-O National Army (PNO/PNA) on 14 June died on 21 June at a hospital.

Around 12 June 2022 (between 10 - 14 June), in Yesagyo township (Pakokku district, Magway region), following the mine attacks against the military forces, two military columns raided refugee camps and abducted five people including a CDM school principal, a CDM pregnant teacher and a 27-year-old CDM nurse and two members of Northern Yesagyo Guerrilla Force as guides on 10 June. On 14 June, the bodies of those five people were found burned alive near Thin Kat village at around 1 pm.
On 9 June 2022, in Kale town (Kale township, Kale district, Sagaing region), military forces arrested the owner of Tun Clinic, accusing him of buying weapons for PDFs.

On 7 June 2022, near Na Lel Inn village (Katha township, Katha district, Sagaing region), a combined force of Katha PDF and Shadowless Guerrilla Force attacked three military vessels traveling to Katha with a rocket launcher at around 4 pm. The military vessels were attacked twice and there were unspecified number of casualties. Locals said ambulance arrived at Katha's Tha Phan port and carried those injured.

Around 4 June 2022 (between 3 - 5 June), in Tha Yet Pin village (Kani township, Yinmarbin district, Sagaing region), the body of a 30-year-old refugee man who was arrested by the military on 3 June while returning to his home was found tied up near the Tha Yet Pin hospital on 5 June.

On 2 June 2022, in Si Mee village (Saw township, Gangaw district, Magway region), about 60 military soldiers from No (25) military's defense equipment factory stormed the village and arrested four men and one woman. One of the arrested men was tortured and died during the interrogation and he worked as a secondary supervisor participating in the Civil Disobedience Movement at the Saw Town People's Hospital. the NUG reported that a health worker who was arrested on 31 May 2022 died after being tortured by the military at an interrogation camp on June 2.

records.actor: Taw Gyi Mway Hauk Tatphwe: King Cobra Force
records.notes: On 1 June 2022, near Min Htein village (Palaw township, Myeik district, Tanintharyi region), the combined forces of the King Cobra Force, Ghost Ranger - Palaw UG, and the Tanintharyi Guerrilla Force (TGF) attacked a military convoy with mines at around 9 am and both sides exchanged fires afterwards. A military truck was destroyed during the fight. The Myanmar Pressphoto Agency reported that there were 4 fatalities and 7 injuries on the military side. The Network Media Group (NMG) reported that four soldiers died at the scene and two more died at the People's Hospital in Palaw town and five others were seriously injured. Fatalities coded as 6, following the more recent estimate of the NMG.

On 29 May 2022, in Okpho town of Okpho township (Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), the People's Defense Force-Thayarwady district Okpho Battalion (3802) Unit-4 shot dead a 45-year-old retired policeman and alleged member of Pyu Saw Htee near Ayemyathayar Hospital. He was selling Korean BBQ at the time he was shot.

On 24 May 2022, near Pyinoolwin town of Pyinoolwin township (Pyinoolwin district, Mandalay region), a 38-year-old Par Ae villager was shot by an unidentified armed group at around 9 am. He was injured and taken to the Pyinoolwin district Hospital.

On 20 May 2022, in Te Gyi village (Mingin township, Kale district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military used 7 aircraft to conduct airstrikes and sent 100 military soldiers to set fire to the houses in the village at around 11 am. There were 2 civilian injuries. Khit Thit Media reported that a pregnant woman and a nurse died. At least 15 villagers were injured. Fatalities coded as 2 based on Khit Thit Media.

On 18 May 2022, in Taunggyi town (Taunggyi township, Shan-South state), military forces arrested the married CDM doctors at around 2 am when they arrived at the Sat San Htun general hospital for emergency surgery.

On 16 May 2022, in Muse town (Muse township, Muse district, Shan-North state), around 20 unidentified armed men beat and shot a civilian when he tried to escape in Swan Saw ward at around 11 pm. The civilian was taken to the hospital. The reason for the attack was unknown and no group has claimed responsibility. Locals claimed it was a clash between pepople's militia members.

On 11 May 2022, in Yangon - Mayangone (Yangon-West district, Yangon region), an unidentified armed group attacked the No. 3 ward administration office on Kyaikwaing Pagoda road at around 10 am. A female head of 100 households was killed on the spot after being shot in the head and a member of the Fire Auxiliary Brigade died on the way to the hospital. The ward administrator was severely injured.

On 5 May 2022, in Hlaingtharya (Yangon - Hlaingtharya-East district, Yangon region), 30 Myanmar military soldiers arrested 1 CDM nurse from a private hospital in No.5 Ward on the Bogyoke road at around 9:40 pm.
On 5 May 2022, in Naung Pat village (Homalin township, Hkamti district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military arrested a CDM obstetrician at her clinic around noon.

On 1 May 2022, in Kyaik Inn village, Thanlyin township (Yangon-South district, Yangon region), the Myanmar military forces arrested a health worker and a nurse that practiced traditional medicine for unknown reasons.

On 26 April 2022, in Mandalay - Mahaaungmyay (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), military force and men in civilian uniforms (assumed to be military soldiers and police) arrested a doctor who is the owner of Thuriya Clinic along with his father and another female doctor by accusing them of allowing CDM doctors to work. The military also sealed off all three branches of the clinic.

On 13 April 2022, in Yangon - Tamwe (Yangon-East district, Yangon region), security forces (assumed to be military soldiers and police) found three suspects and started shooting when they were stopped for questioning by security forces on Pho Sein road in Natmauk ward. Three of them were then arrested with two pistols, 16 rounds of 9mm ammunition and three hand grenades. One of them, who was injured at the time of his arrest, died on the way to the hospital. After investigation, three remote-controlled mines were seized from the house of one of the detainees on 5th Street, Thidar Lane in Hpawt Kan ward, Insein township. According to a statement from the State Administration Council, they were allegedly arrested for setting fire to a KBZ Bank ATM and a YBS No 20 vehicle, including the explosion at Harry's in Myanmar Plaza.

On 7 April 2022, in Thandaung town (Thandaunggyi township, Hpa-An district, Kayin state), the Thandaung PDF detonated a bomb against the military and police forces who came to collect the dead body of the administrator who was targeted earlier on the same day. Two military soldiers were severely injured. One, a captain, died later at the military hospital. Fatility coded as 1.

On 6 April 2022, in Kawlin town (Kawlin township, Kawlin district, Sagaing region), the military alleged that a local PDF launched 40mm artilleries towards the township administration office and an hospital, destroying some staff housings and injuring a civilian.

On 3 April 2022, in Ta Nee Lar Le village of Demoso township (Loikaw district, Kayah state), the military fired heavy arilltery shells at the village, injuring at least three locals and damaging five houses and one clinic. The military also fired heavy arilltery shells at Daw Say and Daw Ta Ne villages. There were no casualties.
On 23 March 2022, in Sin Thay village (Hkamti township, Hkamti district, Sagaing region), military forces in two vehicles arrived and arrested a CDM medical doctor at about 9 am.

Land seizure: On 23 March 2022, in Yangon - Sanchaung (Yangon-West district, Yangon region), military forces sealed off the house of a former editor of Yangon Khit Thit and Min Veterinary Clinic on Myint Mo street.

On 21 March 2022, near Maung Kone village of Tigyaing township (Katha District, Sagaing Region), the Special.9-People's Defense Force attacked the military vehicle at night with mines, killing a military medical doctor and a sergeant. The group attacked the military troops and police who arrived on motorcylces to check the earlier mine attack with remote-controlled mines, injuring five military troops. The military detained two civilians after mine attacks against its troops.

On 18 March 2022, in Thayarwady town of Thayarwady township (Thayarwady district, Bago-West region), the Myanmar military forces arrested a 45-year-old nurse who joined the civil disobedience movement at her residence for alleged member of PDF.

On 14 March 2022, in Daw Se village of Demoso township (coded as Demoso) (Loikaw district, Kayah state), the military fired artillery rounds into the village at around 1 am, destroying a village clinic. The KNDF battalion-09 reported that some villagers were also injured.
On 10 March 2022, in Thayarwady township (Bago district, Bago-West region), military forces arrested a female dentist from Htun dental clinic at about 9:30 pm for unknown reasons.
On 9 March 2022, in Thet Kei Kyin village, Bilin township (Thaton district, Mon State), the Myanmar military forces arrested two members of BPLA and a medical doctor in Thet Kei Kyin village.

On 8 March 2022, in Bhamo town, Bhamo township (Bhamo district, Kachin state), a homemade mine exploded near the fence of the hospital staff rooms in Nyaung Pin ward, destroying the windshields of two vehicles and some windows in the staff rooms. There were no casualties.

Property destruction: On 5 March 2022, in Chaung Ma (West) village of Kani township (Yinmarbin district, Sagaing region), the military set 13 houses, 6 cow barns, 3 farming machines, 3 oil millers and a village clinic on fire.

On 24 February 2022, near Nan Mei Khon town of Demoso township (Loikaw district, Kayah state), the military conducted airstrikes at least nine times, injuring some local defense force members, civilians and medics, destroying 17 houses. The explosions killed a KDF member and broke a villager's leg according to Khit Thit Media.
Property destruction: On 22 February 2022, in Kyauk Tan village (Kawlin township, Kawlin district, Sagaing), about 100 military troops entered a hospital and set fire to medicines and motorcycles.
Property destruction: On 21 February 2022, in Mindat town (Mindat township, Mindat district, Chin), the military troops destroyed the Vum Thu Maung hospital and facilities inside.

On 18 February 2022, in Ywar Thit Gyi village of Singu township (Pyinoolwin district, Mandalay region), the military arrested six locals including a nurse participating in CDM and a NLD youth coordinator.
Property destruction: On 18 February 2022, in Vawmm'tu village of Mindat township (Mindat district, Chin state), the military set the supplies of local clinic on fire.

On 10 February 2022, in Pindaya township (Danu Self-Administered Zone, Shan-South state), a man was hit by four shots fired by unknown assailants while he was playing the Myanmar traditional drum and died at hospital. A local said he was known as a military informant and responsible for multiple arrests.

Around 7 February 2022 (between 1 - 8 February), in Kale town (Kale district, Sagaing region), a 53-year-old man who was arrested on Nat Chaung Road near Chan Myay Hospital by the military troops and Pyu Saw Htee members on 1 February died during interrogation on 8 February and his family members were informed to retrieve his body in the morning.

Around 5 February 2022 (as reported), in Hpa-An town (Hpa-An township, Hpa-An district, Kayin state), military forces arrested a female doctor for posting and writing on her Facebook page for inciting to destabilize the country such as supporting NUG, CRPH, or PDF. The military forces also arrested a 55-year old man in ward 5 for the similar reason.

On 2 February 2022, in Shar Hla village of Seikphyu township (Pakokku district, Magway region), military forces entered the village and arrested locals. Houses and a clinic that provided a free medical care were set on fire.

On 30 January 2022, in Yangon - South Okkalapa (Yangon-East district, Yangon region), the military arrested a father of the owner of Lan Clinic in Ward 13 as they could not locate the son who is accused of calling a silent strike on Facebook.

Around 29 January 2022 (as reported), in Yangon - Mayangone (Yangon-West district, Yangon region), the military arrested a 28-year old man and a 28-year old doctor/owner of Myat General Clinic in ward 2 for posting on thier Facebook accounts about the silent protest.

On 25 January 2022, in Dawei town (Dawei district, Tanintharyi region), the Myanmar military forces raided and arrested seven health workers who volunteered at a charity clinic located on Ye Thein Road in Wei Kyun Nyaung Yan ward of Dawei town. The military also seized an ambulance, medications and medical appliances at around 8pm.

On 24 January 2022, in Dawei town (Dawei township, Dawei district, Tanintharyi region), 7 medics were arrested by Myanmar military in a rented house. Out of the arrested, 6 medics were released days later whereas 1 medic was charged with Section 52 (A) of the Counter-Terrorism Law, sentenced for 5 years on 30 March 2022.

Property destruction: On 22 January 2022, in Kan San Pya village of Mingin township (Kale district, Sagaing region), the military forces and Pyu Saw Htee raided and destroyed six houses of NLD supporters and a government clinic. They looted money, items and motorcycles.

On 18 January 2022, in Tei Taw village, Tigyaing township (Katha district, Sagaing region), the Tigyaing PDF attacked a convoy of two military trucks at around 5:49 pm. One of the cars and some troops were hit with explosives. The military based in Tigyaing town hospital then fired two heavy rounds at the area. Casualties unknown.

On 16 January 2022, in Oo Bo Shwe Gyin ward, Mandalay - Aungmyaythazan (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), an unidentified armed group carried out a grenade attack against military security guard at the northern gate Oo Bo Prison administration training center. The military returned fire. The attack injured a military warrant officer and two others, according a local. Khit Thit reported that an explosion occurred at the guardhouse of the Mandalay Traditional Medicine Hospital near Shwe Kyin toll gate. The military fire about 50 shots in return, according to locals. Khit Thit reported a soldier and a police were killed in the explosion. Fatilities coded as 0 given the latest Myanmar Now report.

On 15 January 2022, in Dwei Loe village of Mutraw (Hpapun) township (coded as Hpapun) (Hpapun district, Kayin state), the military forces launched airstrikes against a medical facility, destroying Ler Htoo Poe hospital. There was an unspecified number of casualties.

On 12 January 2022, in Hpapun township (Hpapun district, Kayin State), the Myanmar military forces carried out airstrikes against Karen National Liberation Army (KNLA) Brigade-5 controlled township of Dwei Loe in Hpapun district at around 1:05 am. The airstrikes destroyed Wa Kha De hospital building.
records.actor2: KNU/KNLA: Karen National Union/Karen National Liberation Army

records.notes: On 5 January 2022, in Ho Nar village (Namhkan township, Shan-North, Shan state), following the earlier clash with TNLA fighters, the Myanmar military fired artillery targeting TNLA fighters operating near the hospital in Namhkan town. Shwe Phee Myay reported that six villagers in Ho Nar village, including three young children and two elderly women in their sixties were injured by the shelling. One of the injured children was less then one year old and another victim was 66 year. Shops and monasteries in Ho Nar and Pang Hto Len villages were also damaged from the shelling.

Property destruction: On 5 January 2022, in Shiit village of Mindat township (Mindat district, Sagaing region), the military destroyed a public health clinic and health support. They also set a residential house on fire.

On 4 January 2022, in Hpapun township (Hpapun district, Kayin state), military airforce jets dropped a total of eight bombs on the villages of Maw Kaw Law and Wah Ka Dar, injuring three people. Homes in Maw Kaw Law and a clinic at Wah Ka Dar were destroyed.

On 24 December 2021, near Mo So village (Hpruso township, Loikaw district, Kayah state), the military's LIB 108 of brigade 66 arrested, burned and killed more than 35 fleeing people including two employees of Save the Children, four children and two women. The Karenni Human Rights group later confirmed five bodies were female including a 12-year-old. The group also reported that victims were tortured before they were burned to death as they recorded signs of the victims being tied up, having their skulls bludgeoned and being stabbed in their chests. The victims included two employees of Save the Children, a staffer participating in CDM, a health worker working for ethnic armed groups, four Mo So villagers, a petrol shop owner and his seven staff, a member of a people's militia and several others in six vehicles and five motorcycles. The military also arrested and shot four members of the KNPLF dead who went to negotiate after the people asked them for help during the arrest of 35 local people by the Burmese army. Fatalities coded as 39.

Property destruction: On 23 December 2021, in Vawmm'tu village, Mindat township (Mindat district, Chin State) the Myanmar military forces fired at a local hospital and damaged the building for unknown reason.

On 20 December 2021, in Mandalay city (Mandalay district, Mandalay region), the military arrested a midwife. She was released again but her uncle was arrested. The military has been targeting and investigating CDM health workers, including nurses.

On 14 December 2021, in Lay Kay Kaw town (Myawaddy township, Myawaddy district, Kayin state), more than 200 military troops from the 560th Light Infantry Battalion raided the town for the eighth time, arrested about 32 people and attacked some of the arrestees by tying them, beating them and pushing their faces into water. The 32 arrestees include the Myaungmya township NLD member of parliament at the Pyithu Hluttaw, the Eainme township NLD member of parliament at the Amyotha Hluttaw, a doctor, and two civil servants who have participated in the Civil Disobedience Movement. The military also confiscated two firearms. DVB, Myanmar Now, Irrawaddy reported that on 28 November 2022, the military sentenced two NLD members of parliament to 26 years in prison each.

On 13 December 2021, in Nyaungdon town (Nyaungdon township, Maubin district,Ayeyarwady region), the military raided the office of the Piti Youth Charitable Association and confiscated an ambulance and arrested four young people. They also arrested a man doing charity work at No. (4) Ward and another young man. The reason for the arrest is still unknown.

On 9 December 2021, in Taunggyi town of Taunggyi township (Taunggyi district, Shan-South state), about 30 forces of the military and police arrived at a shop near Sao San Tun hospital and arrested about 20 young people.

On 8 December 2021, in Ye-U township (Shwebo district, Sagaing region), about 30 military troops raided the Aryu pharmacy and arrested a doctor, who is the daughter of the owner, participating in the civil disobedience movement and her husband.

Around 8 December 2021 (between 6 - 9 December), in Magway township (Magway district, Magway region), a Baptist Church Pastor who was first arrested on 6 December 2021 died in Magway hospital on 9 December after being held in military custody and tortured in Sidoktaya military base for alleged member of Chinland Defense Force.

On 7 December 2021, in Yangon - South Okkalapa (Yangon-east, Yangon), the military troops raided the 'Min Clinic' between Poonami and Post Office on Thitsar Road and arrested the nurses. The reason for the arrest is still unknown.
On 5 December 2021, in Mawlamyine township (Mawlamyine district, Mon state), about 20 military soldiers arrested a doctor at the Ngwe Moe hospital, accusing him of buying the NUG bond.

Land seizure: On 24 November 2021, in Nyaung Kone village (Hlegu township, Yangon-north, Yangon), the military raided and cordoned off a house of second year nursing student. The military has been targeting and investigating CDM health workers, including nurses.

records.actor: Military Forces of Myanmar (2021-) Border Guard Force
records.notes: Looting: Around 23 November 2021 (as reported), in Hpa Laung Taung village of Hpa-An township (Hpa-An district, Kayin state), the BGF broke into the clinic and took medicines. The BGF also destroyed some properties.

On 22 November 2021, in Loikaw town (Loikaw township, Loikaw district, Kayah State), the military raided a Catholic church named Christ the King Church and arrested 18 volunteers, including doctors and nurses, working at the clinic. They were released on 23 November. The military claimed that CDM civil servants and those who were charged with 505 (a) of Penal code are among the detainees.

records.actor: Military Forces of Myanmar (2021-) Border Guard Force
records.notes: Land seizure: On 17 November 2021, in Hpar Lin Taung village of Hpa-An township (coded as Hpar Lin Taung Thu) (Hpa-An district, Kayin state), the BGF troops raided and seized the hospital following the fighting between the BGF and the NKU brigade 1. Patients and hospital staff had to flee.

On 17 November 2021, in Na Gar Bwet village (Kale township, Kale district, Sagaing region), fighting broke out between military forces and the Kalay PDF from 10:30 am to 12 noon. During the clash, the military used medics, who were arrested during the military's raid on the Kalay PDF Battalion 3 camp on 16 November, as human shields. Casualties unknown.

records.actor: Military Forces of Myanmar (2021-) Border Guard Force
records.notes: Property destruction: On 16 November 2021, in Hpa Laung Taung village (Hpa-An township, Hpa-An district, Kayin state), the Border Guard Force under the Myanmar military destroyed the village track hospital and took away medicine.

On 16 November 2021, in Taikkyi township (Yangon-North district, Yangon region), chairman of the Young Men's Buddhist Association (YMBA) was shot in the morning and died at the Mingaladon Military Hospital with gunshot wounds.

On 16 November 2021, near Kale town (Kale township, Kale district, Sagaing region), the military forces attacked and seized the Kale PDF Battalion 3 camp with small arms and killed two male members and one female medic from the PDF's medical team. Nine other female ethnic Chin medics were arrested. The dead bodies of two male members and one female medic were found on 18 November. On 24 November, six of the nine Chin women were taken to Kale Prison. The other three were being held at the interrogation center at the Kale Regional Command Military Headquarters and one of them had been hospitalized with injuries, according to the Kalay PDF. Around 15 October 2022 (month of), the Kale prison court sentenced those nine Chin medics to 12 years each in prison, according to RFA and Myanmar Now.

records.actor: CGYDF: Chindwin Galon Yazar Guerrilla Defense Force
records.notes: On 16 November 2021, in Salingyi township (Yinmarbin district, Sagaing region), two police vehicles were attacked with landmines near the 16-bed village track hospital on the Monywa-Pathein road. Two injured policemen were sent to Salingyi hospital. Attack was carried out by a group of alliances and members of the Chindwin Galon Yazar led by Yebaw Naungdawgyi. Casualties unknown.

On 13 November 2021, in Kamma town, Pakokku township (Pakokku District, Magway Region), the Myanmar military council arrested 2 Health Workers (Medical Doctors), a Woman and a Man, who have joined CDM. They ware arrested while traveling to Mindat, Chin State. Five young people who fled the fighting Mindat were also arrested at 9:32 am.

Around 8 November 2021 (between 6 - 9 November), in Kungyangon township (Yangon-South district, Yangon region), a man who was arrested by military forces on 6 November was tortured in military interrogation and died with injuries on the way to Yangon hospital.
On 5 November 2021, in Hlaingtharya (Yangon - Hlaingtharya-East, Yangon-North, Yangon region), a female nurse was shot twice and killed by unknown assailants at around 10am.

On 5 November 2021, in Hlaingtharya (Yangon - Hlaingtharya-East, Yangon-North, Yangon region), following the earlier killing of a nurse, a young man was arrested by military forces. He was blindfolded and taken to the Ward No. 7 office.

On 29 October 2021, in Myothit town (Magway township, Magway district, Magway region), five military soldiers guarding a gate and two plain clothes military soldiers on a motorcycle exchanged fire at the Magyi Cho Kyay Ywar road near the Myo Thit Public Hospital. A local said that two plain clothes military soldiers on a motorcycle were also guarding the gate at the same time last night when there was an argument between them. One of the two plain clothes military soldiers on a motorcycle was killed and the other was seriously injured. Five military security guards were unharmed.

On 28 October 2021, on Monywa-Budalin highway (Budalin township, Monywa district, Sagaing region), a military convoy carrying an engineer from the Mytel company, was hit by a landmine, injuring the engineer's left arm, leg, abdomen, left shoulder and right arm. He was submitted to military hospital in Monywa.

On 24 October 2021, in Yangon - Dagon Myothit-North (Yangon-East district, Yangon region), the Myanmar military troops opened fire randomly at Panglong hospital and Panglong housing in the evening, killing a passenger on a taxi.
On 23 October 2021, near Rialti village (Falam Township, Falam District, Chin State), the Myanmar military shot and killed a medical doctor working for the Chin National Front.

On 19 October 2021, in Kanbalu town (Kanbalu township, Kanbalu district, Sagaing region), the Kanbalu UG shot a member of the pro-military Pyu Saw Htee group who is a son of the Kanbalu ward 1 administrator, a member of the USDP. He held an event condemning the CDM doctors in front of the 100-bed hospital on March 1. He was hit by a bullet and got wounded.

On 14 October 2021, in Seik Khun village of Shwebo township (Shwebo district, Sagaing region), the military inspected some houses and arrested a doctor participating in the civil disobedience movement (CDM) along with a nurse and an unknown woman.
Around 10 October 2021 (between 9 - 11 October), in Tigyaing township (Katha district, Sagaing region), at least 20 locals including a doctor were arrested by the military forces.
Property destruction: On 3 October 2021, in Min village of Yesagyo township (Pakokku district, Magway region), the Myanmar military troops raided and destroyed a local clinic.

Around 1 October 2021 (month of), in Zay Yar Thi Ri township (Oke Ta Ra district, Nay Pyi Taw), a detainee died from severe injuries and wounds while receiving treatment at military hospital. He was arrested on 2 May and had been tortured by the military for five months. He died on 1 October. His mother witnessed bruises, broken ribs and injured body organs on his body.
On 26 September 2021, in Yangon - Sanchaung (Yangon-West district, Yangon region), the wife of a CDM doctor who was killed by Myanmar military on 25 September was arrested by Myanmar military forces.

On 26 September 2021, in Hman Taw village, Khin-U township (Shwebo district, Sagaing region), Myanmar military forces raided the village and arrested two villagers and one CDM medical staff. The military then killed all three of them.

On 24 September 2021, in Me Yone Ka Lay village of In Ga Po village tract (Kyaikto township, Thaton district, Mon state), a man was shot and wounded by two unidentified gunmen on a motorcycle near an electric transformer. According to locals, he was shot in the waist and was taken at the Kyaikto Township People's Hospital for treatment. The reason for the act was not known.

On 24 September 2021, in Monywa town, Monywa township (Monywa district, Sagaing region), the Myanmar military raided a traditional Kachin restaurant in Chan Myawaddy ward and arrested three men, including a doctor who had previously provided treatment to those wounded during the anti-coup protests.

Around 22 September 2021 (week of), in Dawei town (Dawei Township, Dawei District, Tanintharyi Region), the Myanmar military raided a house where an anti-coup defense group were hiding. The military arrested 23 defense group fighters. The military beat and tortured them. 2 members died in the military custody and 6 members later died at the hospital. Total 8 fatalities coded.

On 18 September 2021, in Ohn Taw ward 1, Taungdwingyi town (Magway district, Magway region), a 50-year-old man who was captured and tortured by the military troops a night before died at the hospital. The reason for the arrest is unclear.

Around 17 September 2021 (as reported), assumed to be in Magway region, junta forces (Coded as military and police) arrested three volunteers who were delivering medical supplies to a volunteer doctor who had been assisting IDPs living in camps in Mindat town. The state-controlled media also reported that medical equipment including one portable X-ray machine, one anaesthesia machine, an oximeter and medicine were seized.

On 8 September 2021, in Kawa township (Bago district, Bago-East), an assistant township education officer who has been participating in the civil disobedience movement was arrested by security forces (coded as military and police) using an ambulance.

On 8 September 2021, in Hlegu township (Yangon-North district, Yangon region), the Myanmar military raided Metta Social Relief Organization Office at night, and arrested four aid workers and took two ambulance cars.

On 24 August 2021, in Yangon - Thingangyun (Yangon-East district, Yangon region), security forces (coded as military and police) raided an apartment in Sabal Phyu Condo. A young man reportedly jumped off the building during the raid. Several people - including the man who jumped off the building - were taken away. Irrawaddy reported that the man who jumped off the building was taken away by an ambulance.

On 21 August 2021, in Taikkyi town, Taikkyi township (Yangon (North) District, Yangon Region), in Mingalar Ward, security forces raided a house on Court Road and arrested four people. They also took away rice, food items and medical supplies.
Property destruction: On 21 August 2021, in Vawmm'tu village of Mindat township (Mindat district, Chin state), soldiers raided the village, destroyed properties, and ate the village nurse's food.

On 16 August 2021, in Tigyaing township (Katha district, Sagaing region), five unknown gunmen shot and killed two factory workers from Tagaung nickel plant. Both died at the Mya Taung hospital. It was reported that the nickel plant was invested in by the Chinese government. There have been a number of attacks targeted at Chinese-linked businesses since the coup.

On 13 August 2021, in Shwebo town (Shwebo Township, Shwebo District, Sagaing Region), the security forces (assumed to be the military and police) arrested a former military medical doctor. The former medical doctor provided coronavirus vaccination to civilians in the town before he was arrested.

On 13 August 2021, in Budalin town (Budalin Township, Monywa District, Sagaing Region), the Myanmar military arrested a medical doctor and a court office clerk who joined the civil disobedience movement against the military coup. He was beaten during the arrest.

On 7 August 2021, in Tha Pyay Kone village of Myanaung township (Hinthada district, Ayeyarwady region), a villager who was arrested and detained by the Tha Pyay Kone police died later on the same day at a hospital from severe injuries. His whole body was found full of bruises and wounds from torture. Two other villagers who were arrested together with him on the same day have been released.

On 1 August 2021, in Momauk township (Bhamo district, Kachin state), the township administrator was shot while on his way home after meeting with ward administrators. He was the former Hlaingtharya Township Administrator and believed to be responsible for the Hlaingthaya massacre. He died on the way to Bhamo hospital. One other ward administrator also injured.

Around 29 July 2021 (between 19 July - 8 August), in Oo Bo prison in Mandalay - Aungmyaythazan (Mandalay District, Mandalay Region), a CDM doctor who was arrested on July 19 on suspicion of being connected to CRPH/NUG died from coronavirus and due to lack of access to medical treatment.

records.actor: Military Forces of Myanmar (2021-) People's Militia Force
records.notes: On 27 July 2021, in Nawnghkio township (Kyaukme District, Shan-North), the People's Militia Force hit an aid worker in the chest with the butt of a rifle. The aid worker has been providing support to coronavirus patients. Health workers and aid workers who have been providing support to COVID-19 patients privately have been targeted by the military regime in recent weeks.

On 26 July 2021, near Bant Bway (coded as Bant Bway (Ywar Ma)) village (Yinmarbin Township, Yinmarbin District, Sagaing Region), security forces (assumed to be military and police) arrested an ambulance driver from a charitable organization who was driving a pregnant woman to Monywa Hospital. He was arrested at the Bant Bway checkpoint.

On 21 July 2021, in Htaukkyant, Mingaladon township (Yangon-North district, Yangon region), Myanmar military forces arrested a volunteer doctor treating coronavirus patients. The military regime has been targeting health workers who are providing support to COVID-19 patients privately.

On 19 July 2021, in Yangon - Dagon Myothit-North (Dagon Myothit-North, Yangon-East district, Yangon region), the security forces (assumed to be the military and police) tricked and arrested five volunteer doctors. The security forces lured them to a house under false pretence with a fake coronavirus emergency case. Once three doctors arrived at a house they were directed and the security forces arrested them. Afterwards, the security forces raided their office and arrested two more doctors. Total five doctors were arrested.

On 16 July 2021, in Mandalay city (Mandalay region), the security forces (assumed to be the military and police) beat and arrested a medical doctor. The military regime has been targeting health workers who are providing support to coronavirus patients privately.

On 13 July 2021, in Mandalay city (Mandalay region), the security forces (assumed to be the military and police) beat and arrested a medical doctor along with his cousin. Later, his cousin was released.

On 11 July 2021, in Dawthponeyan town (Momauk township, Bhamo district, Kachin state), the military LIB 142 based in Dawthponeyan and the battalion 88 based near a village clinic fired at least 10 rounds of artillery shells at the surrounding villages including Kone Law village. One villager was hurt, three animals expired and cars and houses were destroyed in some parts.

On 4 July 2021, in 19th Ward in Myingyan town (Myingyan district, Mandalay region), the ward administrator was shot and died at the hospital. Zero Guerrilla Force announced that they are responsible for the attack, and claimed that the administrator was a member of Pyu Saw Htee group and a military informant.
On 2 July 2021, in Shan Inn village, Kalewa township (Kale District, Chin State), a female nurse participating in Civil Disobedience Movement was arrested by Myanmar military forces.

On 2 July 2021, between Sat Pyar Kyin and Kyi village (Tabayin Township, Shwebo District, Sagaing Region), about 400 Myanmar military soldiers confronted with the Tabayin PDF. The Myanmar military fired at least 40 artillery shells.
