Four Myanmar military soldiers were killed and seven soldiers were injured, while 26 PDF fighters were killed, according to the Tabayin PDF (an earlier report stated that at least 18 PDF members were killed while more than 10 were injured). One PDF member was arrested. After the PDF retreated on Friday, local medics reported that 41 bodies had been found. Total fatalities coded as at least 41 (assuming other reported fatalities are part of the bodies retrieved). Mizzima reported that six university students and two high school students were among those who were killed by the military.

Around 30 June 2021 (between 29 - 30 June), in Pin Ta Le village (Wundwin Township, Meiktila District, Mandalay Region), an NLD village tract chair who was arrested on 29 June was reportedly beaten by military forces stationed at Pin Ta Le hospital. He died the next day on 30 June, although the military claimed that he had died from COVID-19.

On 27 June 2021, in Yangon - Dagon Myothit-East (Yangon-East District, Yangon Region), the head of 100 households was shot by unknown motorcyclists while he was at a betel nut store at the corner of Kyan Sitthar and Yarzathagyn streets. He died from his gunshot wounds while on the way to the hospital.

On 23 June 2021, in Chipwi town (Chipwi Township, Myitkyina District, Kachin State), a female nurse was arrested at her home by security forces (assumed to be military and police forces) and taken into custody at the Chipwi police station. Her phone had also been confiscated. It was reported that she has been participating in the civil disobedience movement since February.

On 23 June 2021, in Myawaddy town (Myawaddy township, Myawaddy District, Kayin State), the head of 100 households was shot by an unidentified gunman in Ward 2 while he was sitting in front of his house to give alms to the monks. He died while on the way to the hospital.

On 21 June 2021, in Yangon - Kyeemyindaing (Yangon-West District, Yangon Region), an office driver working at the General Administration Department office was stabbed several times by an unidentified man about 20 yards from the office. The man fled the scene following attack. The driver was said to be in the hospital and remained unconscious.

Looting: On 17 June 2021, in Chaung-U township (Monywa District, Sagaing Region), military forces stormed several villages (including Myint village, Shwe Ku village, Ywar Thit village) and ransacked shops and houses of protest leaders and health workers participating in the civil disobedience movement. They removed and destroyed NLD signboards. They also broke into the homes of business owners to demand bribes. They also raided a shop and took away 200,000 kyats, cooking oil and a can of kerosene.

On 13 June 2021, in Mandalay city (Mandalay Region), the security forces (assumed to be the military and police) arrested a lecturer and medical doctor from Mandalay Medical University who joined the civil disobedience movement against the military coup. He later died from coronavirus at Mandalay General Hospital on 8 August.
On 11 June 2021, in Myingyan town (Myingyan District, Mandalay Region), a gynaecologist from Myingyan Hospital was arrested by Myanmar military forces.

On 10 June 2021, in Ngathaingchaung town (Yegyi Township, Pathein District, Ayeyarwady Region), three bombs exploded at three different places, including inside a hospital, in front of a bank, and at the general administration department office - at around 8:30pm. Shortly after the explosion, soldiers arrived and fired gunshots and take up security at the hospital. No casualties reported.

On 8 June 2021, near Hpayarthonesu town (Kyainseikgyi Township, Kawkareik District, Kayin State), an unidentified gunman shot a 32-year-old female candidate from the MUP three times with AK-47 firearm in the evening. She later died in the hospital at night. The party stated that the attack was believed to be politically motivated. The MUP has come under criticism since the coup for its decision to join the State Administration Council.

On 2 June 2021, in Yangon - Shwepyithar (Yangon-North District, Yangon Region), five daily-wage workers who were working at a construction of the Mingalardon Military Hospital were arrested by security forces (coded as military and police). One of them later died on 3 June, reportedly due to torture by the security forces.

On 30 May 2021, in Bagan town (Nyaung-U Township, Nyaung U District, Mandalay Region), regime forces (coded as military and police) raided the wrong house and shot and killed a man living in the house. It was reported that regime forces were given false information by a military informant. During the raid, regime forces arrested the man who was shot as well as three others. The man's family was later told to collect his body from Nyaung-U Hospital.

On 27 May 2021, in Meiktila town (Meiktila Township, Meiktila District, Mandalay Region), a police officer was shot by unknown individuals in front of the Meiktila Hospital. He later died while on the way to the military hospital.

On 26 May 2021, in Magway town (Magway Township, Magway District, Magway Region), a surgeon from Magway Teaching Hospital was shot by military forces after he was stopped by the military while on the way back to the hospital staff quarters. He lost his right eye. It was reported that the surgeon was no longer participating in the civil disobedience movement after returning to work.
On 26 May 2021, at Ayetharyar gate near Taunggyi town (Taunggyi District, Shan-South State), 5 men from Nyaung Shwe town on their way to the hospital were arrested by Myanmar military forces.

On 17 May 2021, in Nyaung Khar Shey village (Waw township, Bago district, Bago-East), soldiers arrested a doctor and nurse on duty at a private clinic. This came after a bomb exploded at a high school in the village.

Around 14 May 2021 (as reported), in Pyin Oo Lwin town, Pyin Oo Lwin township (Pyin Oo Lwin district, Mandalay region), a Burmese language tutor died while held in custody at the Pyin Oo Lwin military hospital. He was first arrested by security forces during a crackdown on anti-coup protest on 2 May, where he was reportedly shot and injured in his leg. His family was not able to retrieve his body and have yet been officially notified by the military about his death.

On 14 May 2021, in Zivita Dana Hospital in Mandalay - Aungmyaythazan (Mandalay District, Mandalay Region), security forces (coded as military and police) raided the hospital and arrested 4 healthcare workers and 1 patient and 1 civilian.

On 13 May 2021, in Kyaukpadaung town (Kyaukpadaung Township, Nyaung-U District, Mandalay Region), four homemade bombs exploded in various locations, including at a school, a public hospital occupied by military forces, a municipal office. Casualties unknown.

On 12 May 2021, in Myitkyina township (Myitkyina District, Kachin State), an IOM staff member was arrested by regime forces (coded as military and police) and is believed to be detained in Myitkyina Prison. He has been charged with sedition and incitement as the regime accused him of putting a picture of the military's Commander-in-Chief in a demeaning position and compiling a list of health workers who were not participated in the civil disobedience movement on his mobile phone.

On 9 May 2021, in Thanlyin township (Yangon (South) District, Yangon Region), security forces (coded as military and police) raided three apartments in Star City housing complex, arresting 16 people, including two women and some minors. A medic died after reportedly falling off an apartment during the raid. AAPP reported that there were reports that the medic was pushed off the building by security forces after he refused to be arrested.

On 5 May 2021, in Yangon - Bahan (Yangon-West district, Yangon Region), two to three bombs exploded at a gym in Utopia Tower near Kandawgyi Park. A famous mixed marital arts fighter was injured during the explosion. State media claimed that he was testing a handmade bomb when the explosion happened and that an improvised pipe bomb was found in the vehicle that took the fighter to the hospital. A woman was arrested following the incident.

On 5 May 2021, in Mandalay - Aungmyaythazan (Mandalay district, Mandalay Region), two ethnic Chinese Myanmar nationals were shot by military forces in their car in front of Workers' Hospital after receiving COVID-19 vaccination at the hospital. A woman was shot and killed, while a man was wounded. Military forces also fired four shots at a motorcyclist who reportedly raised the three-finger salute, injuring two people. Military-run media claimed that the woman was accidentally killed by a stray bullet as they were firing towards the motorcyclists who threw a homemade explosive at military forces. An eyewitness reported that soldiers were trying to arrest two delivery drivers but they did not throw explosive devices.

On 5 May 2021, in Yangon - North Okkalapa (Yangon region), three other youths were arrested by military and police forces in relation to the explosion that took place at Utopia Tower earlier on the same day. The mixed martial arts (MMA) fighter who was injured in a bomb blast was later arrested by security forces at the North Okkalapa Hospital for allegedly manufacturing improvised explosives. Security forces then raided the house of the MMA fighter and arrested two people.

On 3 May 2021, in Yangon - Thaketa (Yangon-east District, Yangon Region), the military-appointed ward administration of West Ward No. 7 was stabbed and killed by three unidentified men who entered his home at night. He died on the way to the hospital. In addition, some people were beaten and arrested for making calls to security forces to report on people carrying out pot-banging activities.

On 2 May 2021, in Min Yat village, Launglon township (Dawei district, Tanintharyi Region), a 20-year-old man who refused to stop his motorbike was shot three times by the military troops at night. He died in hospital on the following day.

On 2 May 2021, between 69th Street and Sein Pan Street, Mandalay - Chanmyathazi (Mandalay district, Mandalay Region), security forces in plainclothes arrested civilians and raided a tea shop. A youth was also arrested on 58th Street (Between 116 and 117 Streets). Arrests with the use of cars with ordinary licence plates and ambulances were also reported.

Looting: On 1 May 2021, in Kyat Pyin ward, Mogoke town (Mogoke township, Mogoke district, Mandalay Region), Myanmar military raided Ruby Yadanar Clinic and took 1.5 million Kyats worth of medical supplies. Ruby Yadanar Clinic is a temporary charity clinic.

On 28 April 2021, in Muse town, Muse township, Muse district, Shan-North State, two laboratory technicians at the Kaung San Hospital were arrested by State Administration Council forces. It is not known where they were held or why they were arrested.

On 18 April 2021, in Maubin township (Maubin District, Ayeyarwady Region), military and police forces arrested four doctors who were members of the civil disobedience movement against the Myanmar military coup. A volunteer with a charity was also arrested. All five of them were charged under Section 505(a) of the Penal Code.

On 14 April 2021, in Bhamo town (Bhamo Township, Bhamo District, Kachin State), a striking doctor (also the owner of Ocean Mobile Mart) was arrested by military and police forces. He was one of four health workers in Bhamo who had been charged by the military junta for involvement in the civil disobedience movement.

On 13 April 2021, in Ye town (Ye Township, Mon State), the Myanmar military arrested the head of Ye Town Public Hospital at night. He had been charged under Section 505(a) of the Penal Code by the military junta for his involvement in the civil disobedience movement.

On 12 April 2021, in Tamu town, Tamu Township (Tamu district, Sagaing Region), the Myanmar military and police raided the town and launched an hours-long assault starting in the morning. Gunfire was reportedly heard for at least six hours as security forces destroyed barricades set up by residents to stop them from entering the town. RPGs were reportedly used twice by security forces, and also fired indiscriminately and killed a seven-year-old girl while she was walking to a shop to buy snacks and arrested three young men. Security forces raided Grace Hospital and destroyed equipment, beat up staff and confiscated their phones. They also searched a Methodist church compound and searched and looted phones and money from houses belonging to Gurkha ethnic people, and a local ward administrator's home was also raided. A young man was also reportedly detained and tied to a lamppost on a street after being accused of having a gas-powered gun. More than 200 residents have fled their homes and are in hiding.

On 10 April 2021, in Myeik (Tanintharyi), a doctor who is participating in the CDM was arrested while providing medical treatment to those in need on mobile clinic. Another person who was helping the doctor and also supporting the CDM was also arrested.
On 9 April 2021, in Tedim Town (Falam District, Chin), security forces arrested a nurse who is participating in CDM and her brother. Two other civilians were also arrested.

On 8 April 2021, in Hpa-an town, Hpa-an township (Hpa-an District, Kayin State), a female paediatrician who runs a paediatric clinic in Ward 1 was arrested by plain clothed police officers. While the reason for her arrest was not reported, it was believed to be linked to the fact that the paediatrician had put up a sign outside of her clinic stating that she would refuse to provide services to those who are not supporting the civil disobedience movement.

Property destruction: On 7 April 2021, in Yangon City (Yangon Region), a series of small explosions using handmade sound bombs or fireworks (reportedly meant to create noise and cause minimal damage) occurred at least seven locations in the city - including at government buildings, a military hospital and a shopping mall. A fire also broke out in a Chinese-owned garment factory. While the cause of the fire was not clear, there had been separate cases of fire at other Chinese-owned factories in the city. No casualties reported.

On 7 April 2021, near Pyin Oo Lwin town (Pyin Oo Lwin township, Pyin Oo Lwin District, Mandalay Region), the Myanmar military and police raided Asia Ahlinn Yaung Foundation (AHYF), and arrested a driver and a medical doctor from Yangon who visited the foundation. The security forces also searched the medical care centre and student accommodation and looted some money from students. The AHYF has been provided free healthcare service and education for underprivileged people for about more than 10 years.

On 5 April 2021, in Yangon - Sanchaung (Yangon-West District, Yangon Region), police and military forces raided a charity clinic and arrested four volunteer medical staff members, including a volunteer who is a student from Yangon University of Nursing. Emergency medical equipment was also reportedly seized by security forces.

On 3 April 2021, in Yangon - Mayangone (Yangon-West District, Yangon Region), police and military forces stormed a doctor's home and arrested an orthopaedic surgeon (also a professor at University of Medicine, Yangon). He had reportedly been involved in the civil disobedience movement and had been providing medical treatment to those injured during anti-coup protests.

On 2 April 2021, in Bago town, Bago township (Bago district, Bago Region), a doctor-cum-leader of a free medical clinic was arrested by the police and detained at the Bago General Administration Department office. The reason for his arrest was not known.

On 30 March 2021, in Yangon - South Okkalapa (Yangon (East) District, Yangon Region), soldiers and police raided the Tet Lann Hospital at around 2am, beating up security guards and cleaners. Witnesses stated that security forces were kicking people in the face and head with their boots. They also threatened doctors at gunpoint and searched patient wards. CCTV records were deleted by security forces.

Looting: On 25 March 2021, in Gangaw Township (Gangaw District, Magway Region), the Myanmar military arrived at and raided four villages - Pyar village, Shone Shi village, Kan village and Kan Thar village. They searched houses in the village with the help of a local guide. Two villagers were arrested during the raid in Kan village. In Kan Thar village, Myanmar military looted money and other valuable things. They also searched houses of health workers who joined the civil disobedience movement.

On 22 March 2021, at Sa Mar village (Kyaukse Township, Kyaukse District, Mandalay Region), Myanmar police raided the Sa Mar village track hospital. Villagers from Sa Mar, Bon Kwin and Sin Kun attempted to prevent the police from arresting a doctor. Police beat up and arrested 9 villagers.

On 18 March 2021, at Mudita housing complex-2 in Yangon - Insein (Yangon-North district, Yangon Region), the Myanmar military and police fired gunshots at civilians. Some people were injured. They also raided the house of a doctor who treated wounded civilians and arrested the administrator of Mudita housing. During the incident of arrest, an elderly woman intervened but she was kicked.

On 16 March 2021, in Mogoke town (Mogoke township, Pyin Oo Lwin district, Mandalay Region), three plainclothes men (reported as military forces) opened fire on a man on neighbourhood night watch duty at around 9pm. He sustained serious injuries and was beaten and taken away. He is reportedly being treated as a military hospital.

On 12 March 2021, in Pathein town (Pathein township, Pathein District, Ayeyarwady region), a medical medical superintendent of Pathein Hospital was arrested by the police for allegedly refusing to reopen the hospital. His family members stated that he remained uncontactable as of the evening in 12 March.

Arrests: On 9 March 2021, in Hakha town (Hakha Township, Hakha District, Chin State), military soldiers raided the office of a local NGO called Chin Center for Peace & Reconciliation and arrested security guards who were on duty. They also raided a private clinic and arrested a doctor who was allegedly providing medical treatment to injured protesters.

Around 9 March 2021 (as reported), in Yangon - Mingaladon (Yangon-north District, Yangon Region), the Myanmar military killed the founder of the Su vocational school who was arrested in Shwepyithar township on 8 March 2021 (coded separately). The police from Shwe Pyi Thar police station informed his family to inspect his body at the Mingaladon Military Hospital in the afternoon.

Arrests: On 1 March 2021, in Monywa town, Monywa township (Monywa District, Sagaing Region), police and Myanmar military soldiers arrested about 14 people, including 3 doctors and 11 volunteers, while they were operating free mobile clinics. Security forces have targetted health workers viewed as providing support to anti-coup protesters.

records.actor: Military Forces of Myanmar (2021-) People's Militia Force
records.notes: On 17 February 2021, in Hsihseng township (Pa-O Self-Administered Zone, Shan-South), an NLD member who was the former Hsihseng township municipal chair was arrested by the people's militia and transferred to the eastern command then to Taung Lay Lone prison. He later died of COVID-19 at Sao San Tun hospital on 27 August 2021.
